summary: S0 fix: cascade catchup no longer resets watermarks — stops JSONL duplicate writes on every restart
details: Root cause: m1_poller cascade catchup (step 2c) called reset_watermark(sym, dtf) to 0 for all derived TFs before re-deriving 1440 M1 bars. With watermark=0, ALL derived bars passed UDS dedup and were appended to JSONL again. Data: 59,403 duplicates across 3 symbols (3x multiplication factor = 3 restarts). Example: tf_180 part-20260313.jsonl had 1245 lines but only 415 unique bars. Fix: removed reset_watermark() calls — UDS naturally initializes watermarks from disk (last_open_ms). Bars <= watermark dropped as stale/duplicate, bars > watermark committed as genuinely new. Redis priming (step 1) already loads all TFs from disk. Added dedup_derived_jsonl.py cleanup tool.
why: S0: SSOT JSONL files corrupted with duplicate entries on every restart — violates I1 (UDS narrow waist) data integrity
goal: Derived JSONL files contain exactly one entry per open_time_ms — no duplicates regardless of restart count
risks: If derived bars have gaps in the middle (not at the tail), they won't be re-derived. This is acceptable — gaps from crashes are handled by tools/rebuild_from_m1.py
rollback_steps: Restore reset_watermark() loop in m1_poller.py cascade catchup step 2c
notes: 521 tests pass (3 new). Dedup tool: python -m tools.dedup_derived_jsonl --all

summary: S1 fix: journal gated until warmup completes — no signal entries during cascade catchup
details: Root cause: ws_server delta_loop processes committed bars during derive cascade catchup at system restart. Each complete bar triggers get_narrative() → journal.record(). Narrative oscillates rapidly as SMC engine receives bars one by one (ATR unstable 6→27, zones appearing/disappearing). Result: 25 ghost entries in 2.5 min with duration 1-11s and MAE up to 19 ATR. Fix: _warmup_done bool flag added to SmcRunner. False initially, set True at end of warmup(). journal.record() skipped while _warmup_done is False — narrative still computed for UI, just not journaled.
why: S1: ghost signal entries during restart corrupt journal data quality — false trade_entered/trade_exited with impossible metrics (MAE 118 pts, duration 1.1s)
goal: Signal journal contains only signals from live market state after engine fully warmed up
risks: Signals from bars committed between warmup end and system steady state may still be journaled — acceptable because engine state is stable after warmup
rollback_steps: Remove if self._warmup_done guard from get_narrative()
notes: 518 tests ✅ (2 new). Zero diagnostics errors.

summary: S1 fix: FVG height guard — giant FVG zones filtered by max_zone_height_atr_mult * ATR
details: Root cause: _filter_for_display() applied max_zone_height_atr_mult guard only to non-FVG zones (OBs). FVG filtering loop had distance and fill checks but NO height check. Cross-TF injection (H1/H4 FVGs projected onto M1/M5 viewer) amplified the issue — H1 FVGs spanning 100+ pts appeared as massive zones covering half the chart. Fix: added (z.high - z.low) > max_height check in FVG loop, same threshold as OBs. Uses existing config.smc.max_zone_height_atr_mult (4.0). Test updated from pass-through assertion to filtered assertion.
why: S1: enormous FVG zones cover 50%+ of chart area on XAU/USD and BTCUSDT, obscuring price action and violating Clean Chart Doctrine
goal: FVG zones respect same height budget as OB zones — no zone taller than max_zone_height_atr_mult * ATR displayed
risks: Legitimate large FVGs on HTF (news spikes) will be filtered out. Intended behavior — such zones have no practical trading value per ICT doctrine.
rollback_steps: Remove the height guard line from FVG loop in _filter_for_display()
notes: 516 tests ✅. Zero diagnostics errors.

summary: S1 fix: trigger proximity/displacement guards + ADR-0033 Rev 3
details: Root cause: _resolve_trigger_state() returned 'triggered' for ANY CHoCH after zone anchor, regardless of price distance or impulse quality. Two doctrine violations fixed: (1) CHoCH 3.36 ATR from zone → was 'triggered', now 'approaching' (proximity guard: trigger_proximity_atr=3.0). (2) CHoCH without displacement → was 'ready', now 'in_zone' (displacement guard: MS Shift ≠ just BOS). Implementation: 3 new helpers (_find_qualifying_choch, _has_displacement_near, _is_price_proximate) replace boolean _has_structure_aligned(). Trigger matrix updated: ready requires CHoCH+displacement+in_zone; triggered requires CHoCH+displacement+proximate. ADR-0033 Rev 3: documented counter sub_mode, trigger proximity/displacement, new config keys, new invariants N7-N9, updated test matrix.
why: S1: false trigger alerts on LTF cross-TF zones far from price; 'ready' state without displacement violates ICT doctrine (MS Shift requires impulse)
goal: Trigger resolution doctrine-aligned: proximity gates prevent false alerts, displacement gates require impulse for readiness
risks: Stricter trigger → fewer 'triggered'/'ready' states. Intended: reduces false positives. Config-tunable (trigger_proximity_atr, trigger_displacement_window).
notes: 513 tests ✅ (3 new trigger matrix tests). 5-case manual validation confirmed. Zero diagnostics errors.

summary: S1 fix: narrative direction-alignment mismatch — counter-trend mode introduced
details: Root cause: _synthesize_impl() Step 3 mode decision checked only `alignment=='aligned'` (D1==H4) but not whether zone direction matches HTF bias. When HTF=bullish but best zone=ob_bear → headline '🔴 SELL setup ready' + bias 'HTF bullish aligned' = direct contradiction. Fix: direction is now compared to htf_trade_dir (bullish→long, bearish→short). Three sub_mode outcomes: 'aligned' (direction matches HTF), 'counter' (direction opposes HTF, yellow 🟡 headline, warning), 'reduced' (D1≠H4). _build_bias_summary extended with is_counter param. Diagnostic script tools/diag/test_narrative_live.py created for offline narrative testing from disk data.
why: S1: narrative recommends SELL while showing HTF bullish aligned — misleading trade signal, violates trader trust
goal: Narrative sub_mode correctly distinguishes aligned vs counter-trend trades; UI shows appropriate caution level
risks: Counter-trend mode changes headline color from green/red 🟢🔴 to yellow 🟡 — previously 'aligned' counter-trend setups will appear less confident. This is intentional.
rollback_steps: Revert Step 3 in _synthesize_impl() to check only alignment=='aligned' without direction comparison
notes: 510 tests ✅. Zero diagnostics errors. Verified with test_narrative_live.py on XAU/USD data: M15/H1 correctly show counter mode for bear OB with bullish HTF; H4 correctly shows wait (no grade-A zones).

summary: P4: вимкнено aiohttp access_log у ws_server (227k рядків/день шуму)
details: AppRunner(access_log=None) — aiohttp за замовчуванням логував кожен HTTP запит як INFO рядок. При WS + static + health check це ~1/сек = ~86k/день мінімум. Операційні INFO (CONNECT, DISCONNECT, SWITCH, ACTION) лишаються.
why: S2: операційний шум, 26MB/день stderr без корисної інформації
goal: Зменшити ws_server logging ~95% до значущих подій
risks: HTTP request logging вимкнено — дебаг HTTP проблем потребуватиме тимчасового повернення access_log
notes: 510 тестів ✅. Zero diagnostics errors.

summary: P3: D1 duplicate spam виправлено — terminal target TFs тепер буферизуються для overdue-dedup
details: Root cause: _cascade() буферизувала тільки source TFs (ключі DERIVE_CHAIN), але D1/H4/M3 — terminal targets не в DERIVE_CHAIN. Overdue check_overdue_buckets() кожні 60с перевіряв target_buf==None → завжди передеривував той самий D1 бар → UDS відхиляв як duplicate. Фікс: умова буферизації розширена з DERIVE_CHAIN на DERIVE_CHAIN|DERIVE_SOURCE. _BUFFER_MAX_KEEP[86400]=5 вже існував для D1.
why: S2: ~2 duplicate/хв для XAU/USD D1, логовий шум без втрати даних
goal: Усунути повторну деривацію завершених D1 барів
risks: Мінімальний: буферизація terminal targets збільшує RAM ~незначно (5 D1 барів, 100 H4)
rollback_steps: Повернути умову: if bar.tf_s in DERIVE_CHAIN:
notes: 510 тестів ✅. Zero diagnostics errors.

summary: P2: Binance H4/D1 bar_bucket_misaligned — config alt anchors + stale file cleanup
details: Root cause: build_uds_from_config читає root day_anchor_offset_s=82800 (FXCM grid). Binance H4 derive з anchor=0 (midnight). JsonlAppender reject: (open_ms - 82800*1000) % 14400000 != 0. Фікс: додано day_anchor_offset_s_alt=0 та day_anchor_offset_s_d1_alt=0 — select_anchor_offset_for_open_ms() тепер пробує обидва anchors. Видалено 4 stale H4 файли (FXCM grid бари: 19:00, 23:00, 03:00 UTC) щоб уникнути watermark poisoning.
why: S1: 1958 DERIVE_REJECT bar_bucket_misaligned для BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT tf_s=14400, H4 дані повністю заблоковані
goal: Розблокувати Binance H4+D1 derive pipeline з crypto midnight grid
risks: Архітектурний борг: build_uds_from_config не розрізняє per-broker anchors (D-04, потребує ADR)
rollback_steps: Прибрати day_anchor_offset_s_alt та day_anchor_offset_s_d1_alt з config.json
notes: 510 тестів ✅. Zero diagnostics errors. Stale файли: 6 барів видалено (3 BTCUSDT + 3 ETHUSDT).

summary: P1: FXCM tick_publisher heartbeat/stale detection + broker_sidecar failure escalation (I5 fix)
details: tick_publisher: (1) _last_any_tick_wall_ts трекер — записує wall time кожного tick publish. (2) Heartbeat кожні 60с з INFO. (3) Stale detection: 300с без тіків → WARNING TICK_PUBLISHER_STALE_STREAM + break/reconnect. (4) Cleanup попередньої FXCM сесії перед новою спробою (D-04). broker_sidecar: _consecutive_failures лічильник + ескалація: 10 збоїв → ERROR (~5хв), 60 збоїв → CRITICAL (~30хв). .gitignore вже має .env (D-06 ok). 'price_tik' — задокументована wire-level назва (D-08 скасовано).
why: S1: tick_publisher мовчав після reconnect (I5 violation), 876 помилок ORA-499 без ескалації
goal: Degraded-but-loud: stale stream автоматично reconnect, persistent failure ескалюється
risks: Stale threshold 300с жорстко закодований (TODO: config.json). Під час market close може тригерити хибні stale — але reconnect безпечний.
rollback_steps: Прибрати _last_any_tick_wall_ts, heartbeat блок, cleanup блок з tick_publisher_fxcm.py
notes: 510 тестів ✅. Zero diagnostics errors. Коментарі/логи українською.

summary: P1: logger.debug added to 16 truly silent exception handlers across 8 files
details: R_BUG_HUNTER verified 16 except-pass/except-return[] handlers with zero logging in runtime/ + app/. All 16 converted to logger.debug('SCREAMING_SNAKE_FAIL ...', exc_info=True) — debug level keeps noise low, exc_info preserves full traceback for diagnostics. Categories: 11 cleanup/teardown (broker exit, Redis/JSONL close, pidfile unlink, ws.close, stream.close, proc.kill), 4 data-path best-effort (smc warmup extra, session levels wire, numpy dt64 parse), 1 process lifecycle (supervisor kill cleanup). Created tools/diag/log_audit.py for SCREAMING_SNAKE naming coverage audit (74% = 145/197).
why: I5 (degraded-but-loud) violated by 16 silent handlers — exceptions swallowed without trace, invisible in production at 3:00 AM
goal: Zero silent except:pass in runtime/ + app/ — every exception at least debug-logged with traceback
risks: Debug-level logs only visible when DEBUG enabled; intentional tradeoff: cleanup failures rarely actionable but must be discoverable
rollback_steps: Revert except blocks in 8 files to bare pass/return[]
notes: 510 tests pass. Remaining silent handlers: 0. SCREAMING_SNAKE naming: 74% (pre-existing; not in P1 scope). Cross-role: R_BUG_HUNTER corrected DISCOVERY → R_PATCH_MASTER P1 fix → R_REJECTOR verdict.

summary: ADR-0038 Initial Backfill for Virgin Symbols: Phase 2.5 M1 + D1 direct fetch
details: S1: config.json bootstrap.initial_backfill_m1_bars=1440, initial_backfill_d1_bars=180. S2: BinanceHistoryProvider.fetch_m1_range() — range-based M1 fetch with startTime/endTime. S3: M1SymbolPoller.initial_backfill() + Phase 2.5 in _bootstrap_warmup() — detects virgin symbols (watermark_ms=None after Phase 2), fetches M1 history, writes via _ingest_bar→UDS, re-warmups watermark. S4: fetch_d1_range() (interval=1d) + initial_backfill_d1() + Phase 2.5b for D1 direct backfill (180 bars = 6 months). _parse_klines parameterized with tf_s (default=60 backward compat). I1 compliant: all writes through UDS. Idempotent: runs once per symbol lifetime.
why: Virgin symbols (first launch or new broker) get data at 1 M1/min — H4 empty for 4h, D1 for 24h. Phase 2.5 fetches historical data immediately during bootstrap.
goal: New symbols have full M1 + D1 history within bootstrap, enabling cascade derive of all TFs immediately
risks: Provider must support fetch_m1_range/fetch_d1_range (graceful skip if not). Network failure = degraded-but-loud, no crash.
notes: 510 tests pass. ADR-0037 status updated to Implemented. ADR index next number → 0039.

summary: ADR-0037 Binance Futures second broker: provider, ingest worker, tick publisher, supervisor wiring
details: P0: BinanceHistoryProvider (REST klines via python-binance, backoff retry, CandleBar mapping with src='history' + extensions.broker='binance'). Config: crypto_24x7 calendar (always trading), BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT symbol groups, binance section (enabled=false). P1: binance_ingest_worker.py — factory build_binance_ingest_worker() reusing M1SymbolPoller/M1PollerRunner/DeriveEngine with anchor_offset_s=0. P2: binance_tick_publisher.py — WebSocket wss://fstream.binance.com kline_1m stream → Redis pub/sub tick payloads (same channel/format as FXCM). P3: app/main.py — binance_ingest_worker/binance_tick_publisher modes, process categories, 'all' mode gated by binance.enabled. 510 tests pass, 0 new gate violations.
why: XAU/USD market sleeps on weekends; crypto trades 24/7 → always-live data for development and analysis
goal: Second broker for BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT via Binance Futures, reusing existing UDS/derive/preview pipeline
risks: python-binance on Python 3.14 (verified OK). binance.enabled=false by default — activation requires BINANCE_API_KEY/SECRET in .env
notes: ADR-0037 status Proposed (config enabled=false per K5). Alt A chosen: Direct Worker in .venv (no sidecar needed, python-binance works on 3.14). Symbols use passthrough canonical names (no slash).

summary: ADR-0034a reframed as archived snapshot, not deprecated garbage copy
details: Clarified the role of `0034-advanced-market-analysis-tdaa.md`: it preserves a historical snapshot of the attempted P2-P6 implementation state before rollback. It is non-canonical and not maintained, but it is no longer described as a stale/deprecated throwaway copy. Canonical maintained source remains `0034-advanced-market-analysis-tda.md`. This entry supersedes same-day wording in 20260313-002 and 20260313-004 where `tdaa` was described as deprecated.
why: User clarified that ADR-0034a is intentionally retained as a saved record of the pre-rollback state, not merely an invalid duplicate to discard immediately.
goal: Preserve one maintained canonical ADR while accurately labeling ADR-0034a as archive-only historical context.
risks: Archive snapshot can still confuse readers if opened without the header note; canonical pointer remains at the top of the file.
notes: Historical changelog entries are not rewritten; this clarification is append-only and supersedes earlier same-day wording.

summary: Public docs synced to ADR-0016 trampoline reality; ADR-0034 historical log clarified append-only
details: 1) AGENTS.md and README.md no longer describe ADR-0016 as just 'Python 3.7 / 3.11 dual-venv'; they now point to the documented Python 3.14.2 migration incident, Windows trampoline tree-kill rail, and supervisor PID lock. Stale count-style text ('491+ tests', '35 ADR', 'ADR-0001…0010') was replaced with non-drifting wording. 2) Historical ADR-0034 entries from 20260310-001, 20260310-002 and 20260309-011 are not rewritten in place; instead this append-only clarification records that those entries reflect attempted slices before rollback, while canonical truth is the current ADR-0034 status 'Partially Implemented (P0+P1 only)' and deprecated duplicate `0034-advanced-market-analysis-tdaa.md`.
why: AGENTS/README still lagged behind the ADR-0016 incident documentation, and CHANGELOG could mislead readers into thinking ADR-0034 P2-P6 remain implemented if they read historical entries without later context.
goal: Keep onboarding docs aligned with current runtime reality and preserve audit-log integrity while clarifying superseded ADR-0034 claims.
risks: No runtime risk; append-only clarification depends on readers consulting latest changelog entries before historical ones.
rollback_steps: Revert AGENTS.md wording changes about ADR-0016 and stale counters
Revert README dual-venv note and ADR index description
notes: Historical ADR-0034 entries stay untouched by design; clarification is additive to preserve audit trail.

summary: ADR-0016 navigation sync: Appendix C reflected in ADR index and system overview
details: Документаційний drift після переписування Appendix C закрито в навігаційних документах. `docs/adr/index.md` більше не описує ADR-0016 як просто 'Python 3.7, 3.11'; keywords тепер відображають dual-venv, broker_python, Python 3.14 trampoline та supervisor PID lock. `docs/system_current_overview.md` доповнено operational note про Windows trampoline launcher, tree-kill (`taskkill /T`) і `logs/supervisor.pid`, щоб інцидент не губився поза самим ADR.

summary: ADR-0034 status aligned + K3-K6 enforcement rules + ADR-0016 Appendix C + gate_adr_config_sync

Implementation Status table added. 2) ADR-0016 Appendix C: Python 3.14 migration incident (7 type errors, root cause, K3-K6 lessons). 3) Rules K3-K6 added: K3 Zero Diagnostics Gate, K4 Adjacent Contract Update, K5 ADR Status Gate for Config, K6 One ADR Slice = One Verify Gate. 4) X24-X27 prohibitions. 5) L2 rule fixed (dual-venv reality). 6) gate_adr_config_sync.py machine gate: blocks enabled=true for Proposed/Deprecated ADR features.

summary: aione-top v0.8: process tree view, IO counters, M3/D1 freshness, ws_server startable

VERIFY: py_compile all 4 files clean, --once export JSON confirms parent_pid=supervisor for all workers, IO bytes populated, tree connectors render correctly.

summary: Supervisor: process-tree kill hardening для Python 3.14 venv trampoline + PID lock tests
details: PATCH: _kill_tree() тепер безпечно dispatch-ить taskkill /T на Windows і killpg/kill на POSIX без type-check drift; _terminate() вже працює через tree kill; додано тести на Windows taskkill, POSIX killpg, handle close та PID-file lock behavior. VERIFY: pytest tests/test_app_main_supervisor.py -q.
why: Py3.14 venv trampoline залишав orphan worker після terminate(), що провокувало дублікати supervisor children і конкуренцію на Redis queue
goal: Гарантовано вбивати весь process tree та не допускати silent regressions у supervisor lifecycle
risks: taskkill /T залежить від Windows semantics; mixed external launchers поза supervisor усе ще треба контролювати операційно
rollback_steps: Повернути стару POSIX гілку _kill_tree() у app/main.py
notes: Type-check issue for os.killpg/getpgid closed

summary: ADR-0036 integrated accepted errata without changing runtime scope
details: Merged accepted Errata-1 parts into ADR-0036: ShellStage clarified as presentation-only mapping over canonical narrative, optional TF-sync/killzone/replay hooks documented, Thesis Reveal naming adopted, ambient/session rails tightened, concrete performance gates and P-slice verify updated.
why: Move accepted shell corrections out of research draft and into the canonical ADR before implementation begins.
goal: Keep ADR-0036 as the single reviewable truth for premium shell rollout while avoiding UI split-brain semantics.
risks: Optional upstream hooks from ADR-0034/0035 could be mistaken for hard dependencies if implementation ignores their optional nature.
rollback_steps: Revert docs/adr/0036-premium-trader-first-shell.md to the previous Proposed text
notes: ADR status remains Proposed; this patch integrates accepted errata only and does not auto-accept the ADR.

summary: ADR-0036 hardened for BUILD with contracts, rails, and DoD
details: Closed the main BUILD blockers in ADR-0036: explicit TS presentation contracts, null/cap truth tables, replay and Svelte-aligned accessibility rails, ASCII shell wireframe, per-slice acceptance and test strategy, ADR-0034 forward compatibility, and Definition of Done. Added cross-reference from UI spec and synced AGENTS ADR list.
why: Turn the premium-shell ADR from a strong direction document into an implementation-ready canonical spec without widening runtime scope.
goal: Make P1-P2 safe to start while preserving thesis-first intent and preventing UI split-brain semantics.
risks: The tactical strip can still drift toward mini-dashboard behavior if implementation treats optional TF-sync/phase context as co-primary with the thesis bar.
rollback_steps: Remove ADR-0036 reference from docs/ui spec.md and AGENTS.md if desired
notes: Context7 was consulted for Svelte accessibility constraints around interactive elements, keyboard support, and focus-safe interaction design.

summary: ADR-0036 clarified active-view thesis and symbol/TF anchor role
details: Clarified that the thesis bar is relative to the active symbol and active timeframe rather than a global market verdict. Documented that symbol and TF remain explicit left-anchor trader controls in the shell, and added a v1 timeframe-horizon taxonomy for aggressive scalp, tactical intraday, calmer intraday, and context-first views.
why: Prevent product misunderstanding before implementation by making the shell reading model explicit for intraday and multi-symbol trading workflows.
goal: Ensure premium shell work preserves fast symbol/TF switching while making the thesis bar semantically precise for the active chart view.
risks: If implementation overemphasizes horizon labels, the shell could drift back toward utility density instead of keeping thesis first-plane.
rollback_steps: Revert the active-view clarification in docs/adr/0036-premium-trader-first-shell.md and docs/ui spec.md
notes: Clarification only; no runtime scope or API scope changed.

summary: P1 implementation brief moved into canonical ADR-0036
details: Canonized the P1 implementation brief inside ADR-0036 so premium-shell format changes are documented before code starts: exact files, exact helper API, exact verify, active-view thesis rule, timeframe horizon labels, and explicit P1 out-of-scope boundaries.
why: Avoid relying on chat-only agreements for a product-presentation format change with semantic implications.
goal: Make the first build slice executable from canonical documentation alone.
risks: If later code diverges from the canonical brief without updating ADR-0036, implementation drift could reappear despite the stronger documentation baseline.
rollback_steps: Remove section 11 P1 Implementation Brief from docs/adr/0036-premium-trader-first-shell.md
notes: No runtime or API scope changed; documentation hardening only.

summary: ADR-0036 P1 code slice added shell contracts and pure derivation helper
details: Implemented the first production code slice for ADR-0036 without changing RenderFrame or runtime semantics. Added explicit shell presentation contracts to ui_v4/src/types.ts and created ui_v4/src/app/shellState.ts with pure helpers for ShellStage mapping, thesis/tactical/service state derivation, session accent resolution, and timeframe horizon labels.
why: Start premium-shell implementation from the canonical P1 brief while keeping shell logic presentation-only and subordinate to NarrativeBlock.
goal: Make the premium shell executable in UI code with a strict, reusable helper surface before any ChartHud layout rewrite.
risks: Helper labels can still drift from trader-facing language if later UI integration over-customizes presentation strings without updating ADR-0036.
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build passed. Remaining output is limited to pre-existing Svelte warnings in ChartHud.svelte, DiagPanel.svelte, and DrawingToolbar.svelte.

summary: ADR-0036 P2 recomposed ChartHud into thesis-first shell
details: Reworked ChartHud from a single utility row into a three-layer shell aligned with ADR-0036: left anchor controls for symbol/TF, primary thesis bar reading order, tactical strip, and collapsible Thesis Reveal. Wired the new shellState helper into live HUD rendering, added keyboard reveal toggle on N with Escape close and auto-collapse, and removed the prior ChartHud accessibility warning by replacing click-only static interaction with native buttons.
why: Move from semantic helper availability to actual trader-facing shell hierarchy without widening runtime scope or adding new stores.
goal: Make the premium shell visible in the live UI while keeping symbol/TF switching fast and preserving NarrativeBlock as the only semantic truth.
risks: Trader-facing copy inside the thesis bar may still need tuning after live usage review even though the structure is now correct.
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build passed. Remaining warnings are pre-existing and limited to DiagPanel.svelte and DrawingToolbar.svelte.

summary: ADR-0036 P3 normalized App service rail and restored clean UI verification
details: Normalized the top-right App chrome into a secondary service rail by integrating replay into the same rail as theme, brightness, diagnostics, status, and UTC. App.svelte now derives service-rail presentation through deriveServiceRailState() without adding a new store or changing runtime semantics. In the same slice, removed dead toolbar CSS and suppressed non-semantic diagnostics container warnings so the ui_v4 lane returned to zero warnings.
why: Align replay/service/live chrome with the new thesis-first shell and restore a clean verification lane before moving into later interaction work.
goal: Keep the service rail visibly secondary, remove stray replay chrome outside the rail, and preserve one semantic truth across shell and replay presentation.
risks: Service rail emphasis may still need visual tuning after live browser review on smaller breakpoints even though the state wiring is now centralized.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous ui_v4/src/App.svelte top-right bar and replay button implementation
Reintroduce the prior DiagPanel.svelte and DrawingToolbar.svelte warning state if fully reverting this slice
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings.

summary: ADR-0036 P5 unified focus/research mode across chart, shell, and service rail
details: Integrated the existing ADR-0028 displayMode as the single focus/research state for premium shell interactions. ChartPane now exposes displayMode changes to App and a toggleDisplayMode() method, App surfaces the same mode in the service rail as an F/R toggle, and ChartHud consumes the mode only as a presentation hint so tactical/service noise is demoted without introducing a second truth or a new store.
why: Complete the interaction slice by reusing the already-canonical focus/research mode instead of creating a parallel shell-only focus state.
goal: Keep overlay display budget, shell emphasis, and service-rail language synchronized under one existing UI-local preference.
risks: Visual tuning may still be needed after live usage to ensure focus-mode demotion feels intentional rather than merely dimmed on smaller screens.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous ui_v4/src/App.svelte service rail implementation
Remove the displayMode callback/method wiring from ui_v4/src/layout/ChartPane.svelte
Remove focusMode presentation wiring from ui_v4/src/layout/ChartHud.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings.

summary: ADR-0036 browser polish normalized shell copy and mobile layout
details: Applied a browser-driven polish slice after live QA of the premium shell. shellState now humanizes session and trigger labels, preserves domain acronyms, and removes raw runtime phrasing from action copy; ChartHud now formats reveal copy through the same helpers and enforces thesis-first ordering on the mobile breakpoint; App moves the service rail into a safer bottom mobile position so secondary chrome does not compete with the shell header.
why: The first live browser pass exposed real UX defects that compile-time checks could not catch: raw Off_Session phrasing, repetitive trigger language, and mobile ordering that diluted the thesis-first hierarchy.
goal: Keep ADR-0036 trader-facing on narrow screens and in live usage without introducing new UI state or changing runtime contracts.
risks: The bottom mobile service rail may still need replay-active spacing review if replay controls become denser on small screens.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous ui_v4/src/app/shellState.ts label formatting
Restore the previous mobile CSS blocks in ui_v4/src/layout/ChartHud.svelte and ui_v4/src/App.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings.

summary: ADR-0036 redesign slice 1 elevated thesis hierarchy and demoted service chrome
details: Applied the first deliberate visual redesign slice after the structural prototype phase. ChartHud now renders the thesis as a layered editorial plate with explicit Bias/State/POI/Action hierarchy, a calmer verification cluster, and a labeled Context strip so the top shell reads as thesis-first instead of as a utility row. App.svelte simultaneously softened the service rail material, widened its spacing rhythm, and replaced the compact F/R chip with mode text so replay and focus controls read as secondary service chrome rather than as peer controls to the thesis surface.
why: The live product review exposed a clear gap between correct shell semantics and premium visual authority: the UI still read like a prototype HUD instead of the trader-first shell defined in ADR-0036 and docs/ui spec.md.
goal: Move ui_v4 from structural correctness toward a recognizable premium silhouette without introducing new shell state, backend changes, or contract drift.
risks: A browser pass is still needed to tune exact density on mobile and to confirm that the wider service labels do not compete with replay controls on narrow screens.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous thesis-core and verification markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/layout/ChartHud.svelte
Restore the previous top-right rail labels and CSS in ui_v4/src/App.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings.

summary: ADR-0036 redesign slice 2 refined shell copy and deepened reveal hierarchy
details: Applied the second premium redesign slice to the shell without widening runtime scope. shellState.ts now normalizes trader-facing action/context language, maps TF markers like TF1800 into human labels, strips active timeframe prefixes from thesis action copy, and converts off-session phrasing into Outside killzone. ChartHud.svelte deepens the shell material, spacing rhythm, and typography while rebuilding the Thesis Reveal into a stronger editorial brief with a kicker, denser scenario cards, and labeled Trigger/Objective/Risk rows.
why: The first redesign slice fixed hierarchy, but the live shell still leaked technical copy and the reveal still read flatter than the premium trader-first target defined in ADR-0036.
goal: Make the shell read like a deliberate trading brief rather than a diagnostic panel while preserving NarrativeBlock as the only semantic source.
risks: Further polish may still be needed around exact copy tone for specific scenario strings and density on smaller mobile layouts.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous copy formatting helpers in ui_v4/src/app/shellState.ts
Restore the previous reveal markup and CSS blocks in ui_v4/src/layout/ChartHud.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings. Live browser reload confirmed thesis action reads as ChoCH↓ into OB and tactical context reads as Outside killzone.

summary: ADR-0036 redesign slice 3 reframed service chrome and replay as premium study controls
details: Applied the third premium redesign slice without changing shell semantics or runtime wiring. App.svelte now groups the top-right rail into calmer state, style, and meta clusters with explicit labels such as Feed, Mode, Theme, Candles, and Light instead of terse tooling icons. ReplayBar.svelte was rebuilt from a compact transport widget into a softer study rail with Replay study identity, Back/Play/Ahead transport, Cadence control, and Return live exit language. ChartHud.svelte also received a light anchor-control material pass so symbol, timeframe, and favorite controls sit in the same secondary visual language as the revised chrome.
why: After slice 2, the thesis shell read correctly but the remaining top controls and replay panel still broke the premium trader-first illusion by looking like development tooling rather than integrated product chrome.
goal: Make secondary controls feel intentional and editorially quiet so the shell remains thesis-first while replay still feels like a premium study mode.
risks: Further tuning may still be needed for exact density and spacing on narrower mobile layouts once replay is used more heavily in-session.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous top-right service rail markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/App.svelte
Restore the previous transport-style replay bar markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/layout/ReplayBar.svelte
Restore the previous anchor-control styling in ui_v4/src/layout/ChartHud.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings. Live browser pass confirmed Feed Live / Mode Focus clusters and replay mode now renders Replay study with Return live.

summary: ADR-0036 redesign slice 4 reframed diagnostics as a premium review drawer
details: Applied the fourth premium redesign slice without changing diagState or runtime semantics. App.svelte now presents the diagnostics entry as a label-first desk control inside the service rail rather than as a terse Inspect utility button. DiagPanel.svelte was rebuilt into a calmer right-side drawer with backdrop, editorial header, explicit close affordance, softer section cards, and trader-facing section labels including Live service, Frame cadence, Transport, and Notes while preserving the same read-only diagnostic data sources.
why: Even after the service and replay chrome redesigns, the diagnostics trigger and panel still broke the premium product language by reading as an engineering overlay instead of an integrated review surface.
goal: Make the diagnostic surface feel like part of the trader workstation while keeping it secondary, read-only, and operationally useful.
risks: Further density tuning may still be needed if the warnings section grows substantially during volatile sessions or on very narrow mobile layouts.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous diagnostics trigger markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/App.svelte
Restore the previous overlay-style diagnostics panel markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/layout/DiagPanel.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings. Live browser pass confirmed the trigger reads as Desk and the drawer opens as Review desk / Service pulse.

summary: ADR-0036 redesign slice 5 reframed the left drawing toolbar as a premium chart-side rail
details: Applied the fifth premium redesign slice without changing drawing behavior. DrawingToolbar.svelte now renders as a compact chart-side rail with a small editorial header, label-first tool cards for Level, Trend, Zone, and Clear, calmer active styling, and a clearer collapsed Draw badge instead of a bare vertical icon strip. Hotkeys, collapse persistence, and activeTool semantics remain unchanged.
why: After the service rail, replay chrome, and diagnostics drawer redesigns, the left drawing toolbar remained the last obvious chart-tooling surface that still broke the premium product silhouette.
goal: Make the drawing controls feel like part of the workstation furniture instead of a developer-style icon strip while preserving the existing drawing workflow.
risks: A later mobile pass may still need to tune the rail footprint and collapse behavior on tighter viewports.
rollback_steps: Restore the previous icon-strip markup/CSS in ui_v4/src/layout/DrawingToolbar.svelte
notes: VERIFY: npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings. Live browser reload confirmed the new Chart tools / Markups rail and active selection styling.

summary: P2X.6-G1: preview_plane gate rerun
details: Reran tools.run_exit_gates --manifest tools/exit_gates/manifest.json after overlay work to confirm preview_plane (nomix_disk, uds_hotpath, api_splitbrain, tick_schema) remained green; manifest unchanged.

summary: P2X.6-U1 PATCH A: /api/overlay preview endpoint
details: Added /api/overlay that reads UDS.read_preview_window for tf>=300, collapses the returned preview bars into one overlay bar (canonical open/high/low/close/time) and emits meta{plane:'preview',base_tf}, all without touching write paths or applyUpdates.
why: Expose overlay candles for TF>=5m without blending preview and final planes.
goal: Deliver the backend contract for overlay while preserving preview-only invariants.
risks: Additional preview reads, but limited to supported TF values.
rollback_steps: Revert the /api/overlay handler and its metadata.
notes: VERIFY: python -m py_compile ui_chart_v3/server.py; handler is read-only (UDS only).

summary: P2X.6-U1 PATCH B: overlay series + polling
details: Chart adapter exposes overlaySeries with updateOverlayBar/clearOverlay; app.js polls /api/overlay every second for tf>=300, writes results via updateOverlayBar, clears series when symbol/tf/boot_id changes, and keeps overlay updates off applyUpdates so preview/final streams stay untouched.
why: UI needs a separate overlay candle that mirrors preview data without forcing dedup or upserts.
goal: Render the overlay series through dedicated helpers and rate-limited polling.
risks: Polling adds load for high TFs but is confined to preview-enabled ranges.
rollback_steps: Remove overlaySeries helpers and revert pollOverlay/resetOverlayPolling logic.
notes: VERIFY: overlay polling confirmed via manual check; helper uses overlaySeries only.

summary: P2X.7-D1: restore H4/D1 availability вЂ” cold-start catch-up + on-close timing fix
details: DISCOVERY (D1.1): H4 last disk bar=2026-02-09T10:00Z, D1 last disk bar=2026-02-08T22:00Z; M5 tail is fresh; Redis was observed empty. Root causes: (1) cold-start skipped when `_last_saved_base` existed (no catch-up), (2) on-close used double `_last_trading_minute_open_ms` в†’ 1-minute offset that prevented matching bucket close, (3) `expected_last` computed as `ltm(b1 - 60000)` instead of `ltm(b1)`. PATCH (D1.2): (a) cold-start now computes `gap_bars` and fetches minimal `n` to fill gaps (gap-based catch-up); (b) `last_trading_open` set to `anchor_open_ms` (no double-apply); (c) `expected_last` set to `ltm(b1)`; (d) added exit-gate `htf_available` (5 sub-gates) and timing checks. VERIFY (D1.3): `py_compile` OK; timing tests show anchor `alt2=79200` matches H4/D1 close times; `run_gate('htf_available')` в†’ 5/5 OK; repro steps documented below.
why: H4/D1 bars were not being written after cold-starts and on-close detection always failed despite M5 being live.
goal: Ensure H4/D1 bars are available after restart (cold-start catch-up) and in real-time via on-close detection; add a runtime gate to prevent regressions.

summary: M1 Poller: fix FXCM session lifecycle + reconnect
details: Bug-fix: FxcmHistoryProvider.__enter__() (login) never called -> all symbols fail with session not open. Fix: M1PollerRunner now owns provider lifecycle: _try_connect() at start of run_forever(), _maybe_reconnect(cycle_errors) if all symbols fail in a cycle (cooldown 120s), shutdown() on exit. Also fixed main() finally-block for clean session close. Verified live: M1_POLLER_FXCM_SESSION connected=True, 88 M1 + 24 M3 bars committed across 13 symbols, 0 errors.
why: provider was created but never logged in; poll_once caught RuntimeError gracefully but no bars could be fetched
goal: M1 poller connects to FXCM, fetches M1 bars, derives M3, writes to disk via UDS
risks: reconnect may not recover from all FXCM session failures (e.g. auth revoked)
notes: Redis snap empty on first run (priming finds no disk data at startup). Phase 3 C.1 warmup will fix this. Engine_b pattern: composition.py calls provider.__enter__() explicitly.

summary: C.1: M1 Poller warmup вЂ” Redis priming + M1Buffer bootstrap from disk
details: Added _bootstrap_warmup() to M1PollerRunner.run_forever() before FXCM connect. (1) Redis priming: reads disk tail for each (symbol, tf_s) in {60, 180} via UDS.bootstrap_prime_from_disk, writes snap to Redis. (2) M1Buffer warmup: reads last 10 M1 bars per symbol from disk into M1Buffer so M3 derivation works from first cycle. Added warmup_m1_buffer() to M1SymbolPoller. Runner now receives uds + redis_tail_n from build_m1_poller. Live verified: REDIS_PRIME primed_bars=276, WARMUP m1_buffer_loaded=125, SESSION connected=True, first cycle m1_total=5 errors=0.
why: Without warmup: (1) Redis snap empty until bars accumulate, (2) M1Buffer empty so first 2 M1 bars cannot produce M3
goal: M1/M3 data available in Redis + M3 derivation ready from first polling cycle
risks: disk data may be stale if poller hasnt run for a long time; priming budget not enforced (unlike engine_b)
notes: Phase 3 C.1 complete. Redis snap format = single bar event (not array). 66/66 tests pass.

summary: M1/M3 finals bridge to preview ring + /api/bars history for preview TFs. All 13 symbols have M1+M3.
details: Root cause: M1 poller committed final M1/M3 bars to disk+snap+updates_bus (final plane), but UI for preview TFs read only from preview ring (separate Redis keys). Finals never reached the UI. Fix 1 (uds.py): _publish_final_to_preview_ring() called from commit_final_bar when bar.tf_s in preview_tf_allowlist. Publishes final event (complete=True) to same preview_updates ring that UI polls. Fix 2 (server.py): /api/bars for preview TFs now reads history from disk/snap via read_window() (same as final TFs), then overlays preview_curr for the current forming bar. Previously it only read from preview_tail+preview_curr (tick-based, max ~10 bars). Meta plane=preview+history. Verified: all 13 symbols return 83 M1 + 27 M3 bars (HKG33: 5 M1, market closed). Preview updates ring: complete=True src=history (M1) and src=derived (M3). /api/updates: last_complete=True for all active symbols.
why: M1/M3 final bars from poller were invisible to UI due to preview/final plane separation. User reported empty M1/M3 charts despite M1 poller producing data.
goal: All 13 symbols show M1 and M3 in UI. Finals from M1 poller overwrite tick previews (final>preview). History bars available for chart scrollback.
risks: read_window for M1/M3 adds one extra UDS call per /api/bars request for preview TFs. Minimal impact (snap/disk is fast).
verify: py_compile OK. 66/66 tests. Redis: preview ring has complete=True events for all 13 symbols (except HKG33 market closed). /api/bars: 83 M1 + 27 M3 bars. /api/updates: complete=True src=history/derived.
notes: Key insight: preview ring and updates bus are separate Redis structures. For M1/M3 (preview TFs), UI reads preview ring only. Bridge publishes finals to both planes. /api/bars now uses preview+history hybrid: disk/snap for history, preview_curr overlay for forming bar.

summary: Fix BID/MID price mismatch: preview candles now use BID like FXCM History finals
details: DISCOVERY: M1 bars from disk aggregate perfectly to M5 (diff=0.00), data IS correct broker history. Root cause of broken candles: final bars (FXCM History API) = BID (ForexConnect SDK default), tick publisher was set to MID (config tick_stream_price_mode=mid). When final replaces preview, candle jumps by ~half spread (~.15 on XAU/USD). FIX 1: config.json tick_stream_price_mode changed from mid to bid. FIX 2: tick_publisher_fxcm.py payload mid field changed from conditional (price if mode==mid else mid) to always use selected price (price). This ensures preview worker _pick_price reads bid in all modes. VERIFY: (1) config reads bid at root level, _parse_stream_cfg parses correctly. (2) _pick_price(bid,...) returns bid. (3) payload mid=price=bid. (4) Redis last:tick snapshots confirm: NAS100 bid=24730.42 mid=24730.42 (not avg 24731.32). (5) compile OK, 66/66 tests pass. (6) All 5 processes running.
why: Preview candles built from MID ticks, finals from BID history. When final replaces preview, visible jump by half-spread. Breaks visual continuity.
goal: Candles look correct like TradingView: no price jumps when final replaces preview.
risks: None significant. BID is standard for FXCM charting. Existing preview bars in Redis may still have MID prices until TTL expires or new ticks arrive.
rollback_steps: tick_publisher_fxcm.py L251: revert mid field to conditional expression
notes: Market was closed during verification. Full live verification needed when market opens (Sunday 22:00 UTC). Last tick snapshots confirm bid==mid in payload.
repro_steps: 3. Check Redis v3_local:tick:last:* вЂ” mid field should equal bid field
4. Compare preview:curr bars with disk finals for same open_ms вЂ” prices should match
expected_metrics: tick:last:* payload.mid == payload.bid for all symbols

summary: PREVIOUS_CLOSE stitching: open[i]=close[i-1] in /api/bars for TV-like smooth candles
details: DISCOVERY: FXCM get_history() with O2GCandleOpenPriceMode.PREVIOUS_CLOSE chains bars WITHIN one API call, but first bar of each batch retains actual open (FIRST_TICK). M1 poller fetches small batches every 8s -> ~42% of transitions have price gaps (-8 on XAU/USD). FIX: Added _stitch_bars_previous_close() in /api/bars handler. Iterates bars: bars[i].open = bars[i-1].close. Adjusts high/low if stitched open exceeds original range. Applied to BOTH preview TFs (M1/M3) and final TFs (M5+). SSOT on disk preserved (Rule 19: Final ne chipaty). Also removed temp FXCM diagnostic logging from provider.py (added during investigation). VERIFY: (1) M1: 171 bars, 0/170 gaps. (2) M3: 57 bars, 0/56 gaps. (3) M5: 200 bars, 0/199 gaps. (4) py_compile OK, no lint errors.
why: Price gaps between consecutive candles make chart look broken. Previous version (v1/v2 with engine_b) did not have this because larger batches = fewer cross-batch boundaries. M1 poller small-batch pattern exposed SDK limitation.
goal: TV-like smooth candles: every bar opens where previous bar closed.
risks: Stitching modifies displayed open (and potentially high/low) vs raw SSOT. Any analytics reading /api/bars (not disk) sees stitched values. Acceptable: this is standard TV behavior, SSOT preserved on disk.
rollback_steps: Remove _stitch_bars_previous_close function and its calls in server.py
Revert payload from stitched to hist_bars/res.bars_lwc
notes: Standard TradingView approach: display PREVIOUS_CLOSE, store actual. FXCM SDK O2GCandleOpenPriceMode.PREVIOUS_CLOSE confirmed at fxcorepy.O2GCandleOpenPriceMode.PREVIOUS_CLOSE (from forexconnect import fxcorepy). M1 poller restarted as PID 19416 (clean, no diagnostic logging).

summary: FXCM FIRST_TICK + UI stitching: fix M1 chart artifacts (volume alternation + gaps)
details: DISCOVERY: FXCM get_history() with PREVIOUS_CLOSE mode creates pair-wise M1 artifacts: (1) alternating volume вЂ” every other bar has ~7x lower tick volume (avg 543 vs 75, 99 GAP bars vs 99 STITCHED bars in 200-bar sample); (2) inconsistent stitching вЂ” 50% of transitions have price gaps. The alternation perfectly correlates with gap/stitch status: GAP bars have high volume, STITCHED bars have low volume. FIX: (P1) Removed candle_open_price_mode=PREVIOUS_CLOSE from both fetch_last_n_m1() and fetch_last_n_tf() in provider.py вЂ” FXCM now returns FIRST_TICK data (raw BID opens). (P2) Re-enabled ui_stitching_enabled=true in config.json вЂ” server.py _stitch_bars_previous_close() applies uniform open[i]=close[i-1] for all TFs. RESULT: 0 gaps across all TFs (M1/M3/M5/M15/H1/H4/D1). Smooth TV-like candle display. SSOT on disk has honest FIRST_TICK values. KNOWN REMAINING: FXCM M1 tick volume still alternates (FXCM data source characteristic, inherent to their server, not caused by PREVIOUS_CLOSE). This is cosmetic and can be addressed separately (volume smoothing/hiding for M1/M3). USER DIAGNOSIS VERIFICATION: User claimed finals dont reach UI вЂ” DISPROVEN: _publish_final_to_preview_ring bridge (uds.py:605) works, /api/updates shows 6 history + 6 tick_promoted finals alongside 488 preview events.
why: M1 chart had alternating volume comb-pattern and inconsistent gaps caused by FXCM PREVIOUS_CLOSE mode artifact
goal: TV-like smooth M1 chart: 0 gaps, uniform stitching, honest SSOT on disk
risks: FIRST_TICK removes FXCM-server-side stitching; relies fully on UI stitching. Mixing old PREVIOUS_CLOSE bars with new FIRST_TICK bars on disk вЂ” stitching handles both uniformly. M5+ connector also uses FIRST_TICK now (no regressions observed).
rollback_steps: Restore candle_open_price_mode=PREVIOUS_CLOSE in provider.py fetch_last_n_m1 and fetch_last_n_tf
notes: Diagnostic tool tools/diag/m1_volume_pattern.py created for ongoing monitoring. Volume alternation (FXCM inherent) remains: avg high=543, avg low=75. Consider separate PATCH for volume display smoothing.
verify: 0 gaps across M1/M3/M5/M15/H1/H4/D1. 68/68 tests pass. m1_poller m1=4543 m3=1491 err=0. Updates: 488 preview + 6 promoted + 6 history. System 6 processes.

notes: COLD_START_BARS_FALLBACK=2000 with loud warn if TF missing from table
goal: Cold-start short windows, max=20000, no disk hot-path
details: MAX_RENDER_BARS_ACTIVE_BASE=60000 + MAX_RENDER_BARS_BY_TF -> COLD_START_BARS_BY_TF (1m:10080, 3m:3360, 5m:2016, 15m:672, 30m:336, 1h:168, 4h:1080, 1d:365) + MAX_BARS_CAP=20000. getActiveMaxBars() = min(lastLimit || coldStart, 20000). _lastLimitByView tracks per-view actual limit after load/scroll.
why: limit=60000 -> RAM cache miss (len<limit) -> disk hot-path on every switch. Root cause: cold-start = max-window.
summary: P1: COLD_START_BARS_BY_TF + MAX_BARS_CAP=20000 + view-last-limit
risks: Short cold-start windows = less history on first load; acceptable, scrollback covers expansion.

goal: Incremental scrollback in small steps, always clamp to 20000
details: SCROLLBACK_CHUNK_MAX_BASE: 10000->2000. SCROLLBACK_CHUNK_MIN_BASE: 5000->500. SCROLLBACK_TRIGGER_BARS_BASE: 2000->1000. SCROLLBACK_CHUNK_BY_TF: all 5000->1000. DEFAULT_SCROLLBACK_CHUNK: 5000->1000. _lastLimitByView updated after mergeOlderBars.
why: Scrollback chunks 10-20k + global clamp 60k = excessive disk reads + UI payload overhead
summary: P2: scrollback chunks 10k->2k, min 5k->500, base trigger 2k->1k
risks: Slower scrollback expansion; user needs more scroll events; acceptable for performance.

goal: Single API call per user action, no abort storms
details: elSymbol/elTf change handlers: debounce 120ms via clearTimeout/_switchDebounceTimer. Rapid switching coalesces to last change, preventing abort storms.
why: Each switch = loadBarsFull() + abort = disk read; rapid switching = multiple concurrent disk reads
summary: P3: debounce 120ms on symbol/TF switch
risks: 120ms delay before switch; imperceptible for users.
rollback_steps: Remove debounce wrapper, restore direct async handlers

goal: Hard server-side rail: max 20000 bars, tf-aware caps
details: _clamp_limit(raw, tf_s): cap=min(TF_CAP[tf_s], 20000). TF_CAP: 1m=10080, 3m=3360, 5m-1h=20000, 4h=5000, 1d=3650. Warning limit_clamped:raw->clamped in response. Rate-limited log API_BARS_LIMIT_CLAMP (30s throttle).
why: Server has no cap = UI can request any limit; future regression protection
summary: P4: server-side limit clamp (tf-aware + global 20k)
risks: Minimal: caps are generous for typical use. Legacy clients may see clamped responses.
rollback_steps: Remove _clamp_limit, _TF_CAP, _MAX_BARS_CAP from server.py; remove limit_clamped warning injection

summary: HTF Rebuild from FXCM: controlled rebuild tool for H4/D1 bars from FXCM raw history
details: MODE=PATCH. tools/repair/htf_rebuild_from_fxcm.py (~350 LOC): CLI tool for rebuilding HTF bars in SSOT. Fetches bars from FXCM via FxcmHistoryProvider, validates batch (monotonic, no-dup, complete, src=history), appends to JSONL via JsonlAppender. DiskLayer reader dedup picks new bars (last wins via _choose_better_bar when both complete/history/no-ts). Redis refresh via UDS.bootstrap_prime_from_disk. Modes: --dry-run (default) / --commit. Output: JSON+MD reports. Safety rails: _validate_batch (4 checks). Tests: 10 tests (TestValidateBatch + TestBarSummary), all pass. Exit gate: run fxcm_raw_compare after commit, expect mismatch_count==0.
why: fxcm_raw_compare verdict: GO PATCH rebuild (70 mismatch across XAU/USD + NAS100 H4/D1). Price/volume diffs between SSOT bars and FXCM raw history.
goal: Provide controlled, auditable rebuild of HTF bars from FXCM raw source to align SSOT with broker truth.
risks: Appending bars increases JSONL file size (dedup at read time, not write time). Redis refresh requires running Redis. FXCM credentials needed.

details: Server: _infer_d1_anchor_from_h4 (mode of h4.open_time_ms % 86400000), _derive_d1_tv_from_h4 (6 H4 per D1 bucket, partial/calendar_break/midweek_drop), _make_derived_d1_meta (source=derived_h4_tv). Guard: _TV_ALIGN_ALLOWED_TFS={14400,86400}. D1 handler in /api/bars: reuses H4 derive pipeline, infers D1 anchor, aggregates, caches. UI: loadBarsFull appends align=tv for tf_s=86400 (same as H4). Tests: 4 pure-function tests for D1 derive (anchor, aggregation, partial, midweek drop).
why: D1 bars without align=tv show raw FXCM 21:00 boundaries. TradingView opens D1 at 23:00 UTC.
goal: D1 /api/bars?align=tv returns derived view anchored to 23:00 UTC (inferred from H4 data).
risks: H4 derive pipeline fetch budget multiplied by 6 for D1. Cache TTL 5s mitigates.
rollback_steps: Remove D1 elif block in /api/bars handler
notes: 28 tests pass (24 existing + 4 new D1). /api/updates align=tv stays 400.

summary: Fix: htf_tail_sync per-pair resilience вЂ” one symbol error no longer aborts all remaining pairs
details: Bug 1: rewrite_range() inside sync_one() was not wrapped in try/except, so any rewrite error killed the entire provider loop. Bug 2: main loop had no per-pair try/except, so one fetch/rewrite failure aborted all subsequent symbols. Fix: (1) wrap rewrite_range in try/except returning rewrite_error, (2) add per-pair try/except in main loop creating error entry, (3) count rewrite_error and error statuses as fetch_errors in totals. Evidence from report: results had only 2 entries (XAU H4+D1), fetch_errors=1, remaining 24 pairs silently skipped.
why: htf_tail_sync only synced XAU/USD; 11 stale H4 pairs remained untouched because provider loop aborted on first non-XAU error.
goal: All 13 symbols x 2 TFs processed independently; each pair gets its own status in results[].
risks: Minimal вЂ” only error handling change, no logic change.
notes: 16 tests pass. py_compile OK.

summary: P0 fixes: symbol canonicalization on API boundary, flat_bar_max_volume from config, htf_tail_sync totals split, bars_as_is_sweep audit tool
details: (A) Symbol canonicalization: /api/bars, /api/updates, /api/overlay now auto-replace _ with / if canon symbol exists in config.symbols. meta.extensions.symbol_input+symbol_canon added. (B) flat_bar_max_volume: m1_poller reads from config.flat_bar_max_volume (SSOT), default=4, loud warning if key missing. Removed hardcoded _FLAT_BAR_MAX_VOLUME=1. (C) htf_tail_sync totals now split: fetch_errors/rewrite_errors/validation_errors/unexpected_errors. (D) New tools/audit/bars_as_is_sweep.py: read-only /api/bars sweep per symbol x tf x align with JSON+MD report.
why: USD_JPY/XAU_USD etc returned 0 bars (RAM miss due to _ vs / mismatch). flat_bar_max_volume=1 was too aggressive, config had 4. htf_tail_sync lumped all errors into fetch_errors.
goal: All symbols resolve correctly via API regardless of _/. slash convention. Flat bar classification uses SSOT config value. Full per-pair error breakdown in htf_tail_sync.
risks: Symbol canon only activates if canon exists in config.symbols - safe passthrough for unknowns.
rollback_steps: Revert htf_tail_sync totals to single fetch_errors
notes: 28 tests pass. Verified: USD_JPY -> USD/JPY with sym_canon in meta.

summary: Comprehensive diagnosis of 5 live UI regressions (S1-S5) with root causes, PATCH plans, and priority
details: S5: overlaySeries lastValueVisible:true two price labels (1-line fix). S3: clearAll() missing overlay clear + resetOverlayPolling no clear on overlay-overlay switch (3-line fix). S4: HUD price race between applyUpdates and pollOverlay (policy change ~30 LOC). S1: M1 HKG33 watermark duplicate drops during calendar pause (investigation). S2: stream coverage per TF is by-design (documentation gap).
why: 5 visual regressions in live UI, reported by user with logs and screenshots
goal: Documented root causes with actionable P-slices ready for sequential implementation
risks: S4 HUD policy may need iteration; S1 HKG33 calendar sync needs live investigation
notes: Hardcode scan: 377 hits (41 warn). No invariant changes needed. Priority: S5-S3-S4-S1-S2.

summary: Fix S5+S3+S4: overlay price label off, overlay cleared on switch, HUD price single-policy channel
details: S5 (1 LOC): overlaySeries lastValueVisible false вЂ” removes second price label on right scale (chart_adapter_lite.js:275). S3-1 (1 LOC): overlaySeries.setData([]) added to clearAll() (chart_adapter_lite.js:1067). S3-2 (2 LOC): unconditional controller.clearOverlay() at start of resetOverlayPolling() вЂ” no ghost overlay on overlay-to-overlay switch (app.js:2037). S4 (~18 LOC): updateHudInstrumentPrice/updateHudBarPrice policy вЂ” live price from updates/overlay goes to hudInstrumentPrice (always shown), bar close goes to hudBarPrice (shown only if instrument price stale >60s). All 5 previous direct updateHudPrice calls replaced.
why: S5: two price labels on right scale. S3: ghost overlay candle on TF switch. S4: HUD price jumping between different values per TF.
goal: Single price label, no ghost overlays, stable HUD price across TF switches
risks: S4 hudInstrumentPrice TTL (60s) may need tuning if tick stream stops frequently
rollback_steps: Revert chart_adapter_lite.js: lastValueVisible back to true, remove overlaySeries.setData([]) from clearAll()
Revert app.js: resetOverlayPolling restore conditional else-branch clearOverlay, restore direct updateHudPrice calls, remove hudInstrumentPrice/updateHudBarPrice functions
notes: No UDS/Redis/Disk changes. No new timers/processes. Total diff: ~22 LOC across 2 JS files.

summary: S1 FIX: /api/bars for preview TFs (M1/M3) reads from Redis snap instead of stale RAM
details: Root cause: UI reader UDS for preview TFs used ReadPolicy(prefer_redis=False, disk_policy=never) which served only bootstrap-era RAM bars. New M1 finals committed by m1_poller to disk+Redis were invisible to /api/bars. Fix: changed to prefer_redis=True, disk_policy=bootstrap for preview TF code path in server.py. Now /api/bars for M1/M3 reads current bars from Redis snapshot (written by m1_poller via commit_final_bar). Evidence: disk had M1 bars up to NOW, RAM had 15h-old bars; preview ring had final events but /api/bars returned stale data.
why: M1/M3 chart showed 15h gap between bootstrap-era bars and current preview tick
goal: M1/M3 /api/bars serves current data from Redis snapshot
risks: If Redis snapshot is empty/corrupted, falls back to RAM then disk (degraded-but-loud via prime_incomplete warning)
notes: Requires UI process restart. m1_poller writes to Redis snapshot via commit_final_bar._write_redis_snapshot. Redis has 2359 M1 bars for XAU/USD.

summary: S5 FIX: correct lastValueVisible toggle пїЅ overlay shows live price, candles toggle dynamically
details: Previous S5 fix (20260217-002) was wrong: set overlay lastValueVisible=false, hiding live price entirely. User requirement: live price must ALWAYS be visible. Correct fix: (1) Reverted overlay lastValueVisible to true. (2) Added setOverlayActive(active) method that toggles candles.lastValueVisible: false when overlay active (M5-H1), true otherwise (M1/M3/HTF). (3) resetOverlayPolling() calls setOverlayActive to sync label visibility with TF switch. Result: overlay shows live tick price for M5-H1, candles show their own price for M1/M3/HTF. No duplicate labels.
why: S5 previous patch hid the live price label entirely instead of toggling between sources
goal: Live realtime price always visible on price scale, from appropriate series
risks: None significant пїЅ uses existing applyOptions API
rollback_steps: Revert chart_adapter_lite.js overlay lastValueVisible and setOverlayActive; revert app.js resetOverlayPolling changes
notes: JS changes take effect on browser refresh
Data pipeline confirmed working: Redis preview ring has history events (22/2000). Issue was purely in UI rendering (M1) and query method (M5 overlay).
goal: Instant visual replacement of tick bars with broker history (M1) and final bar display in overlay (M5)
details: ROOT CAUSE M1: updateLastBar() in chart_adapter_lite.js had guard 'normalized.time >= lastBar.time' that silently dropped history bars for already-passed minutes. When history event for minute M arrived after tick events for minute M+1 started, bar was dropped. FIX: Added else-branch for time < lastBar.time пїЅ finds bar in lastBarsData by time, replaces it, renders directly via candles.update(). ROOT CAUSE M5 overlay: /api/overlay final check used range-query with disk_policy=never, which _disk_allowed() always blocked пїЅ so has_final was always False. FIX: Changed to tail-read (limit=3, cold_load=True, prefer_redis=True) which reads from RAM/Redis and correctly detects final bars. Additionally, when final M5 bar is found, overlay now shows the real OHLCV+volume instead of removing the bar.
why: M1 bars from broker did not replace tick preview in real-time. M5 overlay never showed final bars because query was always blocked.
summary: Fix M1 history bar live replacement + M5 overlay final bar display
risks: LWC candles.update() for older bars пїЅ tested with v4.1.3, update() matches by time. Overlay showing final bar is a semantic change (overlay was preview-only).
rollback_steps: Revert chart_adapter_lite.js updateLastBar: remove else-branch, restore original guard-only logic
Revert server.py overlay: restore range-query WindowSpec with since_open_ms/to_open_ms, restore has_final check

summary: ADR-0002: DeriveChain M1 -> M3 -> M5 -> M15 -> M30 -> H1 -> H4 (4 phases)
details: ADR for cascading derivation from M1. Phase 0: stabilize M1 (live_recover + tail_catchup + stale detection). Phase 1: M5 derived from 5xM1. Phase 2: cascade M15/M30/H1/H4 from chain. Phase 3: disable engine_b M5 polling. Key evidence: M1 has 680-bar gaps vs M5 2-bar gaps (no live_recover in m1_poller). Exit gate: M1 completeness >=99% for Phase 0.
why: Two independent FXCM sessions, M1 has massive gaps, H4 derived in UI layer (I0 violation), no single source of truth for M1..H4
goal: Single derivation chain from M1, one FXCM session, M1 as SSOT for all TF <= H4
risks: M1 API quality may differ from M5; dual-writer during Phase 2 creates dedup churn
rollback_steps: ADR is documentation-only, no code changes
notes: Phase 0 is immediate next. User deferred market-close bar feature.

summary: ADR-0002: 2 corrections from external review (config SSOT + tail catchup before main loop)
details: Correction 0.1: Config SSOT alignment note added to Section 5 вЂ” Phase 0 backend keys stay in config.json, gap state surfaces through existing UDS set_gap_state() в†’ /api/status, Phase 1+ derived TF must appear in /api/config tf_allowlist. No separate config silo. Correction 0.2: P0.1 table row clarified 'BEFORE main loop' + invariant. Section 6 P0.1 expanded with explicit _bootstrap_warmup() ordering (primeв†’warmupв†’tail_catchupв†’main loop), readiness signal note, engine_b multi-mode reference. Section 7 added deferred decision: readiness signal evolution for Phase 1+. Evidence: engine_b multi-mode sets prime_ready AFTER tail_catchup (L2091-2101). m1_poller has zero readiness concept (no prime_ready calls). /api/config returns window_policy from _build_window_policy_payload (server.py L711).
why: External ADR review identified 2 gaps: (1) config keys could become separate silo from /api/config policy, (2) P0.1 tail catchup timing relative to readiness was underspecified
goal: ADR-0002 corrections ensure config SSOT integrity and explicit bootstrap ordering before m1_poller enters main loop
risks: None (documentation-only change)
rollback_steps: Revert ADR-0002 to previous version (git checkout)
notes: Both corrections verified against codebase: engine_b.py L2091-2101 (multi readiness), server.py L711-758 (/api/config), m1_poller.py L505-544 (bootstrap). User deferred UI config venv for later.

summary: P0.fix: remove calendar gate from poll_once + watermark pre-filter + date_to in fetch (ADR-0002 Phase 0)
details: BUG FOUND: m1_poller had a calendar gate at the top of poll_once() that blocked the entire poll when market was closed. This meant the LAST M1 bar before a market pause (e.g., XAU/USD 21:59 before 22:00 break) was NEVER fetched. engine_b (M5) does NOT have such a gate - it uses calendar-aware cutoff + caught-up check instead. FIX: (1) Removed blocking calendar gate, kept calendar state logging. (2) Added watermark pre-filter: bars <= watermark are filtered before ingest, eliminating UDS stale-drop WARNING spam for instruments with sparse data (HKG33). (3) Added date_to=expected+1M1 to fetch_last_n_m1 call, ensuring FXCM returns bars near the cutoff rather than arbitrary latest bars. VERIFY: 162 tests pass, live test shows all 12 active symbols at 1-2min latency, err=0, cal_skip=0 (was 6), stale=0, recovering=0. +15 LOC (1080->1095).
why: Calendar gate caused 1-bar gap at every market break transition. Watermark pre-filter needed to prevent stale spam for sparse instruments.
goal: Zero missed bars at market break boundaries. Clean logs.
risks: Slightly more FXCM calls during market close (caught-up check prevents actual fetch). HKG33 gap remains (FXCM does not provide M1 data).
rollback_steps: Restore calendar gate in poll_once by re-adding: if not self._check_calendar_state(now_ms): self._calendar_skips += 1; return
Remove watermark pre-filter and date_to from fetch call
notes: Confirmed by reference: engine_b._poll_m5_once() has no calendar gate, uses calendar-aware cutoff only. Same pattern now applied to m1_poller.

summary: ADR-0002 Phase 1: core/derive.py вЂ” DERIVE_CHAIN + GenericBuffer + aggregate_bars + derive_bar + derive_triggers (strict cascade pure logic)
details: РќРѕРІРёР№ РјРѕРґСѓР»СЊ core/derive.py (~310 LOC, pure, Р±РµР· I/O). DERIVE_CHAIN вЂ” РґРµРєР»Р°СЂР°С‚РёРІРЅРёР№ strict cascade: M1в†’M3(3Г—), M1в†’M5(5Г—), M5в†’M15(3Г—), M15в†’M30(2Г—), M30в†’H1(2Г—), H1в†’H4(4Г—). GenericBuffer(tf_s) вЂ” РїР°СЂР°РјРµС‚СЂРёС‡РЅРёР№ Р±СѓС„РµСЂ РґР»СЏ Р±СѓРґСЊ-СЏРєРѕРіРѕ TF (Р·Р°РјС–РЅСЏС” M1Buffer + M5Buffer). aggregate_bars() вЂ” С‡РёСЃС‚Р° Р°РіСЂРµРіР°С†С–СЏ Р· calendar_pause_flat filter. derive_bar() вЂ” РїРѕР±СѓРґРѕРІР° derived Р±Р°СЂСѓ Р· source_buffer. derive_triggers() вЂ” РІРёР·РЅР°С‡РµРЅРЅСЏ trigger buckets РїС–СЃР»СЏ commit source Р±Р°СЂСѓ. Strict cascade: M30=2Г—M15 (Р·Р°РјС–СЃС‚СЊ flat 6Г—M5), H1=2Г—M30 (Р·Р°РјС–СЃС‚СЊ 12Г—M5). 162 С‚РµСЃС‚Рё pass, 0 СЂРµРіСЂРµСЃС–Р№.

Smoke test: 240 M1 -> 80 M3, 48 M5, 16 M15, 8 M30, 4 H1, 1 H4. All 162 existing tests pass. Pre-existing test_tv_mismatch_probe failure (stale import) unrelated.

details: DeriveEngine class (~220 LOC): on_bar(bar)->cascade (buffer->triggers->derive->commit->recurse). commit_tfs_s controls which TFs commit to UDS (Phase 2 default: {180, 14400} = M3+H4). register_symbol_uds() for shared UDS with m1_poller (no file race). Per-symbol threading.Lock for cascade integrity. warmup_bars() for bootstrap. Full cascade: M1->M3+M5->M15->M30->H1->H4. Intermediate TFs (M5-H1) derived in-memory, not committed (engine_b handles). ThreadPool skipped (m1_poller already parallelizes per-symbol).

summary: ADR-0002 P2.3: Wiring DeriveEngine into m1_poller (M1в†’cascade M3вЂ¦H4)
details: 6 changes to m1_poller.py: 1) import DeriveEngine, 2) _derive_engine attr on M1SymbolPoller, 3) _ingest_bar calls engine.on_bar(bar) if engine present else legacy _derive_m3 fallback, 4) M1PollerRunner accepts derive_engine param, 5) _bootstrap_warmup reads 300 M1 disk bars per symbol into engine.warmup_bars(), 6) build_m1_poller creates DeriveEngine with config anchor+calendars, registers shared UDS, injects into each poller. Config key: derive_engine_enabled (default true). Verified: 162 tests pass, smoke test M3=80 H4=1 committed, M5/M15/M30/H1 NOT committed (engine_b handles).
why: Wire DeriveEngine into m1_poller to complete ADR-0002 Phase 2. M1 bars now cascade through DeriveEngine to produce M3+H4 final bars via UDS.
goal: Full M1в†’M3в†’M5в†’M15в†’M30в†’H1в†’H4 cascade wired in live m1_poller process. H4 becomes first-class UDS TF.
risks: H4 derivation activates only after ~4h of live M1 data (warmup fills 300 bars = ~5h for M5 but H4 needs 240 M1). Legacy _derive_m3 fallback if derive_engine_enabled=false.
rollback_steps: Revert 6 changes in m1_poller.py (restore inline _derive_m3 call, remove engine wiring)
Set derive_engine_enabled=false in config.json for soft disable
notes: P2.3 completes Phase 2. Next: Phase 3 (remove H4 derive from UI ~300 LOC).

summary: ADR-0002 P3: Remove H4 derive from UI вЂ” H4 is now first-class UDS TF
details: Removed ~590 LOC from server.py: (1) H4 derive constants (_TV_H4_ANCHOR_REMAINDER_MS, _TV_DERIVED_FROM_TF_S, cache vars), (2) 5 functions (_build_calendar_for_symbol, _derived_cache_key, _derive_h4_tv_from_h1, _make_derived_meta, _derive_h4_tv_with_budget), (3) H4в†’TV auto-redirect in /api/bars, (4) entire align=tv /api/bars handler (~155 LOC), (5) align=tv rejection in /api/updates, (6) unused MarketCalendar import, (7) updated docstring. From app.js: removed align=tv param for H4 requests. From tests: removed 11 H4 derive test functions + 2 helpers (~270 LOC). Kept _ALIGN_TV/_ALIGN_FXCM + align param parsing for backward compat. All 151 tests pass.
why: H4 derivation now handled by DeriveEngine in m1_poller (Phase 2). No need for on-the-fly derivation in UI server. Simplifies server.py, removes split-brain risk, UI becomes pure read-only renderer for H4.
goal: H4 read through standard read_window(tf_s=14400) from UDS. UI server is thin renderer without derive logic. ADR-0002 Phase 3 complete.
risks: H4 data availability depends on DeriveEngine warmup (~4h of M1 data needed). If derive_engine_enabled=false, H4 will show empty until engine_b catches up.
notes: Phase 3 complete. Next: Phase 4 (M5 derived vs broker comparison, 1 week). Pre-existing broken test test_build_instrument_info_fields in test_tv_mismatch_probe.py (unrelated to Phase 3).

summary: Duplicate m1_poller diagnosis: 2 stale processes killed, 2560 disk duplicates removed across 64 JSONL files
details: ROOT CAUSE: two m1_pollers running simultaneously (PID 6664 stale from yesterday + PID 4596 from today) caused UDS watermark corruption. M1 bars dropped as stale/duplicate by UDS never reached DeriveEngine, starving M5+ cascade. Fixed by killing stale processes and dedup of JSONL files via tools/dedup_jsonl.py --fix.
why: M5 derive stalled at 13:30 while M1 was live at 17:00. Duplicate processes caused split-brain on disk SSOT.
goal: Restore healthy single-writer invariant (I5/UDS).
risks: Stale dedup might miss edge cases if duplicate bars had different OHLCV values; kept last occurrence per open_time_ms.
rollback_steps: Restore JSONL from backup if dedup caused data loss
notes: HKG33 has separate gap issue due to market hours.

summary: DeriveEngine warmup fix: load M5/M15/M30/H1 from disk at bootstrap (4512 bars vs 3862 before)
details: DEFECT: warmup_bars() in _bootstrap_warmup() only loaded M1 bars (tf_s=60). After restart, M5/M15/M30/H1 buffers started empty, requiring ~5min for M5, ~15min for M15, ~1h for H1, ~4h for H4 to fill from live cascade. FIX: added _WARMUP_TFS list [(60,300),(300,20),(900,10),(1800,10),(3600,10)] to load intermediate TF tails from disk via UDS.read_tail_candles(). Total bars per symbol: ~350 (was ~300). H1 jumped from 12:00 to 16:00 within 3 minutes of restart (was taking ~70min). Also added DERIVE_SKIP diagnostic WARNING in derive_engine._cascade() for tf>=300 when derive_bar returns None пїЅ permanent operational monitoring.
why: Cold start warmup defect caused H4 to not derive for ~4 hours after any restart, violating SLO targets.
goal: Eliminate cold-start cascade delay. H1/H4 should derive within minutes of restart.
risks: More disk reads at startup (~50 extra reads per symbol, ~13 symbols). Total bootstrap time increase negligible (<1s).
rollback_steps: Revert _WARMUP_TFS changes in m1_poller.py L882-886 back to single (60,300) read
Remove DERIVE_SKIP logging from derive_engine.py L260-275
notes: warmup_bars() filter (bar.tf_s not in DERIVE_CHAIN) already accepts M5(300)/M15(900)/M30(1800)/H1(3600) as DERIVE_CHAIN keys.

summary: m1_poller pidfile guard: prevent duplicate instances (I5 single-writer enforcement)
details: Added _PID_FILE (logs/m1_poller.pid) guard at startup. Uses Windows OpenProcess + POSIX os.kill(0) for cross-platform PID liveness check. Behavior: (1) live PID in pidfile -> ERROR + exit(2), (2) stale PID -> WARNING + proceed, (3) no pidfile -> normal start. Pidfile cleaned up in finally block. ~45 LOC added.
why: Root cause of M5 stall was two m1_pollers running simultaneously. PID guard prevents recurrence.
goal: Enforce single-instance invariant for m1_poller (critical SSOT writer).
risks: If m1_poller crashes without cleanup, stale pidfile remains пїЅ handled by stale PID detection. Windows-specific PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION flag tested.
notes: Tested: live PID blocked with ERROR code=2, stale PID cleaned with WARNING.

summary: aione-top v0.1: htop-like read-only TUI monitor for v3 platform
details: Isolated package aione_top/ with 4 files (~460 LOC). Dependencies: rich (TUI), psutil (OS metrics). Sources: psutil (CPU/Mem/Swap/processes), Redis (v3_local keys/health), disk (JSONL freshness per symbol x TF), HTTP (/api/status). Display: Rich Live with 4 panels пїЅ system header (CPU bars), process table (v3-highlighted), components health (Redis/UI/pidfiles), data freshness matrix (symbol x M1/M5/M15/H1/H4 with color-coded age). Modes: --once (single snapshot) and live (auto-refresh). Read-only: no imports from runtime/core/ui, no writes. Compliant with Dependency Rule (tools-level isolation).
why: Need operational visibility into v3 processes, derive chain health, Redis state, data freshness пїЅ without external monitoring stack.
goal: Quick htop-like view of platform health during development and operation.
risks: psutil/rich are new dependencies. collectors.py does Redis SCAN which may be slow with many keys. No auth.
rollback_steps: pip uninstall rich psutil (if not needed elsewhere)
notes: Level A (read-only) only. Level B (control plane) deferred to separate initiative.

summary: aione-top v0.3: CPU optimization (44x speedup) + zombie prevention
details: v0.2->v0.3 performance fix. Root cause: collect_all() took 2300-4800ms per cycle (3s interval = ~80% CPU). Fixes: (1) collect_processes() two-pass: scan pid+name for ALL procs (3ms), detail query ONLY for ~8 python procs (50ms) вЂ” vs full-attrs scan 2278ms. 29x faster. (2) _read_last_line(): seek-from-end O(1) instead of f.readlines() loading entire JSONL files into memory (78 files per cycle). (3) _TtlCache: heavy collectors (Redis SCAN 10s, disk freshness 10s, HTTP /api/status 10s, pidfiles 15s) cached with TTL. Light (OS, processes) every cycle. (4) swap_memory() cached 30s (was 490ms on Windows per call). (5) Cached Redis connection between cycles (reuse instead of new connection). (6) Zombie prevention: _kill_stale_instances() at startup scans/kills old aione_top processes + writes pidfile + cleanup on exit. Final benchmark: collect_all cold 193ms, warm 52ms (was 4782ms/2302ms).
why: 3 zombie aione_top instances consuming 43-49% CPU each. Collector cycle time exceeded refresh interval.
goal: aione-top uses <2% CPU per cycle. No zombie accumulation.
risks: TTL cache means heavy collectors show data up to 10-15s stale. Acceptable for monitoring.
notes: v0.2 changelog was not written separately (display improvements). This entry covers both v0.2 display changes and v0.3 perf fixes.

summary: aione-top v0.4: interactive mode (kill/cache/pause controls)
details: Level B (control plane) for aione-top. New file: actions.py (~75 LOC) with kill_by_pid (self-protection guard), kill_duplicates, kill_all_v3, clear_redis_ns, clear_app_cache. app.py: keyboard polling via msvcrt (Windows) with Unix select fallback; _UIState state machine (normal/kill/cache/confirm_kill_all modes); _handle_key dispatcher. display.py: # column added to processes table (for kill-by-index), build_footer function with mode-specific hotkey bars. Hotkeys: [k] Kill (1-9 by #, d duplicates, a all v3 with y/n confirm), [c] Cache (r Redis ns, t app TTL), [r] force refresh, [Space] pause/resume, [q] quit. Status messages with 5s TTL. Tested: state machine logic, self-kill protection, footer rendering all modes, --once backward compatible.
why: User needed operational control: kill duplicate/zombie processes, clear Redis cache, manage platform from monitoring tool.
goal: Interactive htop-like control plane for v3 platform management.
risks: kill_all_v3 destroys running platform (behind y/n confirm). clear_redis_ns removes all v3 cache (forces re-prime). No undo.
notes: initiative: aione-top-interactive. New boundary: actions.py = control plane (kill/cache). Exceeds 150 LOC budget as declared initiative.

summary: aione-top: fix false DUP! detection for app.main launcher wrappers
details: Root cause: app.main --mode X (launcher/wrapper process) spawns real worker via subprocess.Popen. Both processes have cmdline containing the role keyword (e.g. --mode m1_poller contains m1_poller). _classify_process checked individual roles BEFORE launcher pattern, so launcher got classified as the role too. Fix: detect app.main + --mode pattern FIRST. --mode all = supervisor (shown). --mode X (individual) = return None (hidden, only real worker shown). Also removed --mode fallback checks from individual role detections (e.g. removed --mode connector check, keep only main_connector). Verified: 6 real workers shown (0 DUP), 5 launcher wrappers correctly hidden.
why: User saw 5 false DUP! processes (26MB launcher wrappers) alongside 5 real workers after restarting platform with individual app.main --mode commands.
goal: Correct process classification: show only real workers, hide launcher wrappers.
risks: None. Launchers are just wait-loops, no useful info lost.
notes: The --mode connector/tick_pub/ui fallback checks were also removed since app.main wrappers are now filtered early. Only direct module matches remain.
