notes: v0.5 initiative: aione-top-pipeline. ~250 LOC additions across 3 files. Tested: --once shows both pages, 151 tests passed.
goal: Visibility into system internals: bootstrap phases, primed data, errors, data flow - all from one tool.
details: New page 2 accessible via [Tab] key. Three panels: (1) Bootstrap and Writer Status from Redis prime:ready + status:snapshot (boot_id, prime readiness 13/13 sym, primed bar counts per TF, writer errors/degraded). (2) Primed Bars per Symbol grid: 13 sym x 8 TF matrix with color-coded bar counts from prime:ready. (3) Recent Events log tail: parses logs/*.log files, filters WARN/ERROR + key milestones (BOOTSTRAP_DEGRADED, M1_POLLER_STATS, GAP_DETECTED, WARMUP, etc), shows with timestamp + source tag + color-coding. New collectors: collect_pipeline_data() (2 Redis GETs), collect_log_tail() (seek-from-end log parsing). TTL-cached at 10s/5s. Page indicator in footer. --once mode shows both pages.
why: S1 error isolation added BOOTSTRAP_DEGRADED warnings but user cant watch logs constantly. Need monitoring of bootstrap health, data pipeline, errors in real-time TUI.
summary: aione-top v0.5: Page 2 Pipeline Monitor (bootstrap status, bars grid, log tail)
risks: Log parsing depends on log files existing in logs/. If platform runs without log files (stdout only), log panel shows tip message. Minimal risk.

summary: aione-top v0.6.1: 5 bugfixes (CPU, freshness, primed totals, layout, Ctrl+C)
details: 5 fixes based on live v0.6 testing: (1) Processes panel compact: removed ST column, narrower PID/RSS/THR/Up columns, fixed size=12 instead of ratio=1 вЂ” Redis text no longer cut. Components ratio 4:3:2 (was 3:3:2). (2) Freshness from publish_time: age_s = now - (open_ms/1000 + tf_s + 1), so M1 candle starts aging from 61st second. (3) Cached bars from per_symbol: bootstrap panel computes totals by aggregating prime_tail_len_by_symbol (correct: M1=35k) instead of stale prime_tail_len_by_tf_s (was M1=713). (4) CPU persistent Process objects: global _proc_objects dict retains psutil.Process between cycles вЂ” cpu_percent now shows real values (was always 0.0 because baseline lost each cycle). (5) Ctrl+C terminal restore: explicit Live.start()/stop() instead of context manager, console.show_cursor(True) + ESC[?1049l alternate screen exit in finally block вЂ” terminal clean after Ctrl+C, no lag on restart. _format_age: <=0 returns ok (was now).
why: Live testing revealed: (a) Redis panel text cut by wide Processes, (b) freshness age misleading вЂ” counting from close instead of publish time, (c) primed total wildly wrong (713 vs 35k M1), (d) CPU always 0.0% вЂ” psutil needs persistent Process objects for delta, (e) Ctrl+C leaves terminal in broken state (alternate screen, hidden cursor).
goal: v0.6.1: stable TUI without visual/data bugs, correct metrics, clean terminal lifecycle.
risks: CPU shows 0.0 on first cycle (baseline) вЂ” expected for psutil. --once mode always 0.0.

Revert app.py: restore with Live() context manager, remove show_cursor/alternate screen cleanup

summary: ADR-0003 S2: Supervisor process isolation вЂ” restart with backoff instead of kill-all
details: S2 implementation: (1) Process categories: critical (connector, m1_poller), non_critical (tick_publisher, tick_preview), essential (ui). (2) Backoff config: critical base=10s max=300s 5 attempts; non_critical/essential base=5s max=120s 10 attempts. (3) Non-blocking restart scheduling: crashed process removed from pool, restart scheduled with delay, executed on next loop iteration вЂ” other processes monitored normally during delay. (4) Stable reset: restart counter resets to 0 after 10min stable run. (5) Critical exhausted (5 crashes in <10min) в†’ supervisor fails loud (kills all). Non-critical exhausted в†’ removed from pool, others continue. (6) _terminate fix: always close file handles even when process already exited (was returning early before handle cleanup). (7) ChildProcess: removed frozen=True, added module field for restart capability. ~85 LOC net addition.
why: F9 from ADR-0003 failure model: any non-zero child exit в†’ RuntimeError в†’ finally terminates ALL 5 processes. tick_publisher WS disconnect (weakest link) в†’ entire platform down. Need per-process restart with isolation.
goal: Non-critical crash в†’ auto-restart with backoff, NOT kill-all. Critical exhaustion в†’ loud fail. Supervisor loop non-blocking.
risks: Blocking during restart delay eliminated (non-blocking schedule). If critical process crash-loops, 5 attempts Г— base 10s = ~5min before supervisor gives up. During that time other processes continue normally.
rollback_steps: Revert app/main.py: restore frozen=True on ChildProcess, remove module field, remove _PROCESS_CATEGORIES/_BACKOFF_CFG/_STABLE_RESET_S/_restart_state/_restart_schedule, restore raise RuntimeError in main loop, restore _terminate early return
notes: S2 complete. 151 tests passed. Backoff verified: non_critical 5sв†’10sв†’20sв†’40sв†’80sв†’120s (cap); critical 10sв†’20sв†’40sв†’80sв†’160s. Next: S3 (unified prime_ready + budget fix).
S3 complete. 151 tests passed (1 pre-existing failure in test_tv_mismatch_probe.py, unrelated). Next: S4 (bootstrap params -> config.json).
goal: UI waits for BOTH connector AND m1_poller readiness. Budget allows full priming. Timeout -> explicit degraded warning, not silent.
details: S3 implementation: (1) redis_snapshot.py/uds.py: set_prime_ready() gets optional component parameter вЂ” key becomes {ns}:prime:ready:{component}. Backward compatible (no component = connector key). (2) m1_poller.py: _publish_prime_ready() after _bootstrap_warmup() publishes prime:ready:m1 via UDS with payload {v:1, ready:true, component:m1_poller, ts_ms, symbols, tfs}. TTL=21600s (6h, same as connector). (3) app/main.py _wait_for_prime_ready(): AND-gate checks both prime:ready (connector) AND prime:ready:m1 (m1_poller). Timeout from config.json prime_ready_timeout_s (default=30). Return value used: False -> UI_START_DEGRADED warning (loud). Structured logging: PRIME_READY_OK per component, PRIME_READY_ALL_OK summary, PRIME_READY_TIMEOUT with ready/missing components. (4) config.json: redis_priming_budget_s 5->15 (was too low: 104 ops x 100ms = 10s > 5s budget -> partial priming). Added prime_ready_timeout_s: 30. ~75 LOC net.
why: F8 from ADR-0003: UI started before m1_poller finished bootstrap. _wait_for_prime_ready checked only connector key, return value discarded. redis_priming_budget_s=5 too small -> partial priming guaranteed.
summary: ADR-0003 S3: Unified prime_ready AND-gate + budget fix
risks: If m1_poller process not started (mode!=all), AND-gate waits full timeout (30s) then starts UI degraded. Acceptable: timeout is explicit + logged.

summary: Calendar-aware derive_triggers: fix H4 19:00 never derived for ALL symbols + overdue bucket safety net
details: ROOT CAUSE: derive_triggers() used nominal last source slot (bucket_end - source_tf_ms) to determine when to trigger derivation. For H4 19:00 bucket, expected_last = H1 22:00. But 22:00 falls in daily break for ALL calendar groups (cfd_us_22_23 break 22-23, fx_24x5 break 21:55-22:30, cfd_eu break 21-07). H1 22:00 is never derived -> H4 19:00 trigger never fires. Bug cascades at ALL TF levels where last nominal source slot is non-trading. H4 19:00 count = 0 for ALL 10+ symbols across ALL dates. FIX: (1) _has_any_trading_in_range() helper checks if any minute in a source slot is trading. (2) derive_triggers() walks back from expected_last to find last slot with trading minutes. (3) DeriveEngine passes is_trading_fn from calendar to derive_triggers(). (4) check_overdue_buckets() periodic safety net (60s) attempts derive for closed-but-missing buckets. VERIFY: Simulation with XAU_USD Feb 19 M1 data: H4 goes from 4 bars to 5 (19:00 appears). 151 existing tests pass, 0 regressions.
why: H4 19:00 was NEVER derived for any symbol due to calendar-unaware trigger logic
goal: All H4 buckets derived when source bars exist; timer-based safety net
risks: Minimal: is_trading_fn is optional param, backward compatible. Overdue check is additive.

why: User requested gap signaling вЂ” display all gaps per symbol (TF, datetime)
summary: GET /api/gaps endpoint: serves scanner report for UI gap visualization
risks: none вЂ” read-only endpoint from static report

summary: Unified disk_policy for all TFs: explicit cold-load + RAM-short disk fallthrough + Redis TTL survives breaks
details: 3 root causes fixed: (1) server.py had split disk_policy: preview TFs=bootstrap, final TFs=never; now ALL TFs use disk_policy=explicit allowing disk fallback when Redis+RAM miss. (2) UDS read_window returned partial RAM data (500 warmup bars) instead of falling through to disk for full set; now when RAM < limit and disk allowed, reads from disk. (3) Redis TTLs were too short (M5=1h, M15/M30=4h) causing key expiry during daily breaks; now: M1/M3=1d, M5-H1=3d, H4/D1=7d.
why: After clearing browser cache, M5/M15/M30/H1 charts were empty because Redis keys expired during market breaks AND disk fallback was blocked (disk_policy=never). M1/M3 worked (preview path had disk fallback), H4/D1 worked (long TTL). User requested unified logic for all TFs.
goal: All TFs use identical read path: Redis -> RAM -> Disk (fallback). No more split between preview and final TFs for cold-load.
risks: Disk reads happen when Redis+RAM both miss, but _read_window_disk populates RAM via set_window so subsequent reads hit RAM. Cold-load disk reads are one-time, not hot-path.
rollback_steps: revert disk_policy to never in server.py L1315,L1412,L1559,L1679
revert UDS RAM-short logic in uds.py
notes: I6 invariant preserved: disk reads only for cold-load/scrollback (user-initiated), not for polling/updates. After first disk read, RAM is populated and serves all subsequent requests.

summary: Deep overdue scan (N buckets) + cascade after overdue derive
details: check_overdue_buckets() had two issues: (1) only checked 1 previous bucket - gaps older than 1 bucket were never retried; (2) overdue-derived bars were buffered but NOT cascaded - overdue M5 would not propagate to M15/M30/H1/H4. Fix: _OVERDUE_LOOKBACK dict (M5=6, H1=3, H4=3), sorted TF processing (bottom-up), cascade() call after each overdue derive. 6 new tests.
why: Single missed M1 killed entire H4 bar propagation. Overdue safety net was too shallow and did not cascade.
goal: Live stability: H1/H4 bars recover automatically within 60s overdue check cycle.
risks: Slightly more work per overdue check (N iterations per TF instead of 1). Mitigated by lookback limits and already-derived check.
rollback_steps: Revert derive_engine.py _check_overdue_for_symbol to single prev_bucket scan
notes: Also ran backfill_cascade --all --date-range 20260214-20260220: 23 new bars written from existing M1 data. Remaining gaps are M1-level (broker data missing). Today 20260220 data 100% clean (7 PASS tail_integrity).

summary: Comprehensive heal: 13 symbols x 7 TFs вЂ” broker M5 fetch + cascade derive + Redis reprime
details: Full data audit and healing operation across all 13 symbols x 7 TFs (Feb 13-20). Three-phase heal: (1) fetch_m5_isolated --all --n 5000 --backfill: fetched 291 new M5 bars directly from FXCM broker (bypasses M1 all-or-nothing derive limitation). Per-symbol: GER30=214, NGAS=48, USD/CAD=15, USD/JPY=12, GBP/CAD=11, NZD/CAD=10, XAG/USD=8, XAU/USD=5, NAS100=4, SPX500=4, US30=4, HKG33=3. (2) backfill_cascade --all --date-range 20260213 20260220: derived 158 new bars (M15=53, M30=51, H1=29, H4=15) from newly fetched M5. (3) UDS bootstrap_prime_from_disk: reprimed 194,437 bars into Redis tails. Result: 0 DATA_GAPS for today (Feb 20) across all 13 symbols x all TFs. Historical gaps reduced from 654 to 110, all remaining are: (a) data-start boundary (Feb 13 first fetch), (b) Presidents Day holiday (Feb 16), (c) break-edge effects. Root causes identified: M1 gaps from low overnight liquidity (NGAS/EUSTX50) cascade to M5 via all-or-nothing derive; broker direct M5 fetch resolves this.
why: User requested comprehensive chart healing for all symbols/TFs before UI release work
goal: All 13 symbols x 7 TFs clean today, historical data healed where possible, root causes documented
risks: Redis reprime overwrites existing tail data; no data loss since disk is SSOT and bars are dedup.
rollback_steps: Redis tails will be overwritten on next system restart anyway (bootstrap_prime_from_disk)
Disk JSONL bars are append-only and dedup-safe
notes: Non-recurrence: (1) Patch 042 ensures live cascade works within 60s overdue cycle. (2) For instruments with chronic M1 gaps (NGAS overnight), periodic broker M5 fetch (cron) is recommended. (3) All 110 remaining historical gaps are non-actionable: data-start boundary, holiday, or break-edge.

summary: P044: 3 fixes вЂ” H4 overlay enabled, 185 flat ghost bars purged, M1 fallback overlay for 5 symbols without tick stream
details: Three-fix patch for chart quality: (1) H4 overlay enabled: OVERLAY_MAX_TF_S 3600в†’14400 (app.js L88), server guard tf_s>=14400 в†’ tf_s>=86400 (server.py ~L1515). H4 С‚РµРїРµСЂ РѕС‚СЂРёРјСѓС” live preview candle РґР»СЏ РІСЃС–С… СЃРёРјРІРѕР»С–РІ. (2) Flat ghost bar purge: СЃС‚РІРѕСЂРµРЅРѕ tools/purge_flat_bars.py (scans JSONL for bars where o==h==l==c AND volume<=threshold). Purged 185 flat bars across 131 files (worst: EUSTX50=49, HKG33=34, GER30=20, SPX500=19). Redis reprimed post-purge (bootstrap_prime_from_disk). (3) M1 final fallback overlay: 5 symbols (EUSTX50, HKG33, NGAS, NZD/CAD, SPX500) have no tick preview from FXCM broker. Added fallback in /api/overlay (server.py ~L1592): when tick preview is empty, reads M1 final bars from Redis tail via UDS, aggregates into target TF bucket. Source tagged as m1_final_fallback, degraded-but-loud warnings: [preview_empty, overlay_fallback_m1_final]. Verified: 13/13 symbols return valid overlay at H1+H4. 157 tests pass.
why: Charts were visually broken: no H4 live candle, flat ghost bars creating artifacts, 5/13 symbols with no live candle at any TF
goal: All 13 symbols show live overlay candle at H1+H4, no flat ghost bars, clean monotonic data
risks: M1 fallback overlay has ~8s latency (polling interval) vs tick-based real-time; acceptable for non-tick symbols
rollback_steps: Flat bar purge is non-reversible (bars were invalid); reprime Redis if needed
notes: FXCM OFFERS table limitation: 5 symbols do not receive tick stream. M1 fallback is best available approximation. Per-symbol calendar configuration still pending as separate initiative.

summary: P-slice 1 DISCOVERY: updates pipeline code correct; root cause = m1_poller not started
details: Full trace of updates pipeline: commit_final_bar (uds.py L619) -> _publish_update (L1276) -> _RedisUpdatesBus.publish (L1905). Architecture correct, events ARE published to Redis. Previous audit diagnosis of 0 events was WRONG: (1) used cursor= instead of since_seq= in API, (2) Redis scan searched XAU_USD (underscore) but actual keys use XAU/USD (slash) via raw symbol. Root cause: user started 4 individual modes (connector, tick_preview, tick_publisher, ui) but forgot --mode m1_poller. The --mode all attempt crashed (exit code 1, SUPERVISOR_CRITICAL_EXHAUSTED). Stale pidfile (PID 26656) does not block restart (_pidfile_guard handles stale correctly). After starting m1_poller: M1=95, M3=31, M5=18, M15=6, M30=2 events in 4 minutes. H1/H4 will appear after sufficient derive cascade runtime. Secondary finding: D1 has 180 events/symbol (flooding) because engine_b commits historical D1 bars via commit_final_bar instead of non-publishing bootstrap path.
why: Updates pipeline flagged as dead in previous DISCOVERY audit; needed root cause before any PATCH
goal: Identify whether updates pipeline has code defect or operational issue
risks: D1 flooding pollutes updates list with historical bars (secondary issue, not blocking)
notes: VERIFY: at 20:48 UTC, M1 last_bar=20:47 (1min ago), M5 last_bar=20:40, M15 last_bar=20:30, M30 last_bar=20:00. H1 expected at ~21:01 UTC. API /api/updates returns 18 M5 events for XAU_USD. Invariants: I0-I4 OK, I5 violated (no loud warning when m1_poller absent). Repro: python -m app.main --mode m1_poller

summary: H4 anchor fix 68400->79200 + purge legacy M5/H4 history + rebuild H4 from H1 + Redis reprime
details: ROOT CAUSE: config day_anchor_offset_s=68400 (19h) produced H4 grid [3,7,11,15,19,23] matching no standard. FXCM winter=22h -> grid [2,6,10,14,18,22]. Also M5 had 173k legacy broker bars (src=history) violating ADR-0002. FIX: (1) config.json: day_anchor_offset_s 68400->79200, ui_warmup_bars_by_tf aligned to >= cold_start. (2) Created tools/purge_rebuild_h4.py: purges M5 src=history, deletes ALL H4, rebuilds H4 from clean H1 data (4xH1 aggregation with new anchor). (3) Executed on all 13 symbols with per-symbol verification. (4) Redis reprime for M5+H4 (26/26 OK). RESULT: M5 total=4395 (0 history), H4 total=4040 (0 history), all H4 at correct hours [2,6,10,14,18,22], monotonic, 0 dups.
why: Broken candles and gaps on all charts, especially H4. Two interleaved anchor grids + legacy broker bars (ADR-0002 violation).
goal: Clean H4 data on correct FXCM winter grid, no legacy history bars in M5/H4.
risks: H4 coverage reduced to time range covered by existing H1 data (~25-50 days per symbol). M5 reduced to recent derived bars only (~370 per symbol). DeriveEngine with new anchor will produce correct new bars going forward.
notes: System restart needed for DeriveEngine to use new anchor. ui_warmup_bars_by_tf increased to match ui_cold_start_bars_by_tf (was causing POLICY_CONFIG_INVALID on UI start).

summary: aione_top: launchers visible + kill_with_launcher + role colors
details: (1) collectors._classify_process: individual-mode launchers (app.main --mode X) return sup:<role> instead of None -> visible in TUI. Before: 6/10 visible; after: 10/10. (2) actions v0.5: added kill_with_launcher(pid,procs) - kills both worker and launcher parent; kill_all_v3 kills launchers first to prevent auto-restart. (3) display: added sup:* dim role colors (cyan/green/yellow/magenta); sup:* rows dimmed. (4) app: hotkey [k]+digit uses kill_with_launcher instead of kill_by_pid. Import updated.
why: Launchers (app.main --mode X) were invisible in aione_top; killing workers only caused auto-restart by launchers
goal: All v3 processes visible in top; killing via top also kills launcher to prevent restart
risks: Minimal - display-only changes + kill improvements. No runtime logic affected.
rollback_steps: Revert collectors.py L199-207, actions.py to v0.4, display.py role_colors, app.py import+kill logic
notes: Verified: collect_processes returns 11 including 4 sup:* + m1_poller

summary: m1_poller: psutil-based pidfile guard vs PID recycling
details: _is_pid_alive() now uses psutil.Process(pid).cmdline() to check if process actually contains m1_poller in cmdline. Prevents Windows PID recycling false positive (OpenProcess returns True for recycled PID of unrelated process). Fallback to OS-level OpenProcess if psutil fails.
why: Stale pidfile with recycled PID blocked m1_poller start (M1_POLLER_DUPLICATE error)
goal: Robust pidfile guard: only block if actual m1_poller is running
risks: psutil import already used in m1_poller; minimal
rollback_steps: Revert _is_pid_alive to ctypes-only version at L1299-1314
notes: Also deleted stale logs/m1_poller.pid (PID 23276 was NoSuchProcess)

summary: aione_top v0.7: restart + start processes from TUI
details: actions.py: (1) ROLE_MODULE SSOT mapping role->module for all 5 startable roles; (2) _ROLE_ALIAS for tick_pub->tick_publisher; (3) restart_process(pid,procs): if launcher exists, kill worker only (launcher respawns with backoff); if no launcher, kill+launch new; if target=launcher, kill both+relaunch; (4) restart_all_v3: kill all + relaunch each role; (5) start_process(role): launch via app.main --mode <role> with --stdio files, detached (CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP+DETACHED_PROCESS on Windows); (6) get_missing_roles: return roles not running. app.py: new modes restart/confirm_restart_all/start; [x]=restart, [s]=start; digit select + [a]=all. display.py: footer keys/badges for restart(orange), start(green) modes.
why: User needs to restart and start processes from aione_top TUI without manual CLI commands
goal: Full process lifecycle management from TUI: kill + restart + start
risks: start_process uses DETACHED_PROCESS which may behave differently if .venv not activated. Mitigated: uses _find_venv_python() to find .venv/Scripts/python.exe explicitly.
rollback_steps: Revert actions.py to v0.5 (remove restart_*/start_*/ROLE_MODULE/STARTABLE_ROLES), revert app.py imports+modes, revert display.py footer keys
notes: Verified: restart connector via kill_worker -> launcher respawn in ~12s. All imports pass. ROLE_ALIAS handles tick_pub->tick_publisher mismatch.

If previous TF was D1 (86400), all M5 bars got string YYYY-MM-DD time -> dedup collapsed them -> empty chart. Fix: moved setViewTimeframe() before setBars() in both loadBarsFull and cache_restore.

summary: P1: Process Inventory вЂ” code-first system documentation (692 LOC)
details: Reverse-engineered from code: all 6 processes (Supervisor, Connector, M1 Poller, Tick Publisher, Tick Preview Worker, UI Server), 3 FXCM sessions, Redis topology, UDS roles, DeriveEngine cascade, bootstrap sequence. 13 sections with file:line evidence. Self-reviewed via sub-agent with ~14 corrections applied.
why: System has no comprehensive process documentation; needed for gap analysis and onboarding
goal: TradingView-class documentation quality for all platform processes
risks: None вЂ” docs only, no code changes
notes: Part 1 of 7-part documentation initiative (P1-P7). 13 key findings at end of doc.

summary: P2: Data Flow вЂ” broker-to-UI data flow mapping (893 LOC, 16 sections)
details: Comprehensive data flow documentation: 8 flows (D1 fetch, M1 fetch, cascade derivation, tick stream, tick preview, UI cold load, UI live updates, bootstrap), UDS commit pipeline 3-way write, Redis topology with exact key patterns/TTLs, preview vs final plane, watermark policy, stitching, TF x Source x Dest matrix. Self-reviewed via sub-agent: 35+ line refs corrected, 3 semantic notes added (UpdatesBus raw symbol, mid=_pick_price, LWC time field). 12 findings including H4/D1 min_coldload>tail_n mismatch.
why: No data flow documentation existed; needed for gap analysis and understanding A->C->B pipeline
goal: Complete evidence-based mapping of all OHLCV/tick data flows from broker to UI
risks: None вЂ” docs only, no code changes
notes: Part 2 of 7-part documentation initiative. Key findings: (1) dual event streams, (2) channel typo price_tik, (3) preview v=0, (4) shared UDS no race, (5) ok=ssot_written only, (6) two M3 derive paths, (7) no disk hot-path updates, (8) RAM cache from updates, (9) boot_id mismatch detection, (10) stitching correct, (11) three Redis clients per process, (12) H4/D1 min_coldload>tail_n.

summary: P3: UDS/Store вЂ” 3-layer architecture spec (1411 LOC, 23 sections, 10 findings)
details: Comprehensive UDS internals documentation: 3-layer architecture (RAM LRU, Redis snapshot, Disk JSONL), 6 dataclasses (WindowSpec, ReadPolicy, WindowResult, UpdatesSpec, UpdatesResult, CommitResult), commit_final_bar guard chain (5 guards), preview plane (throttle, allowlist, bridge), watermark model, disk policy (NEVER/BOOTSTRAP_ONLY/EXPLICIT), UpdatesBus (Redis Lists, cursor_seq), sorted_dedup, factory wiring (create_uds), selftest. Self-reviewed via sub-agent: 19 semantic errors found and corrected (all 6 dataclass definitions, RAM _windows not _store, cache bar format open_ms/close_ms only, UpdatesBus key pattern order, publish(event) single-arg, _disk_allowed signature, JsonlAppender constructor, guard order, _publish_update bar.to_dict()). 10 findings: F1 raw symbol vs symbol_key, F2 logging.error on every non-final drop, F3 RAM max_keys=8 for writer, F4 disk bootstrap 60s window, F5 preview tail no TTL, F6 two dedup paths, F7 legacy l fallback, F8 preview bridge M1/M3 only, F9 no fsync JSONL, F10 selftests in production.
why: No UDS internals documentation existed; needed to understand the SSOT store layer between ingest and UI
goal: Complete evidence-based spec of UnifiedDataStore 3-layer architecture with file:line references
risks: None вЂ” docs only, no code changes
notes: Part 3 of 7-part documentation initiative. Key architectural insights: (1) RAM is pure LRU cache with no persistence, (2) Redis snapshot is write-behind with configurable TTL/tail per TF, (3) Disk JSONL is SSOT with FINAL_SOURCES guard, (4) commit pipeline writes RAM->Redis->Disk in order, (5) reader tries RAM->Redis->Disk with policy-controlled disk access, (6) preview plane is separate from final with 500ms throttle, (7) watermark per (symbol,tf_s) prevents stale writes, (8) UpdatesBus uses Redis Lists not Pub/Sub for persistence.

details: Created P5_contracts_guards.md covering: 4 JSON Schema contracts (bar_v1/tick_v1/updates_v1/window_v1), 16 canonical dataclass types (T1-T16), 11 SSOT constants (K1-K11), 109 guards (G1-G109) across 5 layers (core/runtime-store/runtime-ingest/app/ui), invariant enforcement matrix (I0-I6), 24 exit gates (EG1-EG24), loud vs silent analysis (64 LOUD / 33 SILENT), 7 findings (F1-F7). Self-review score 7/10 в†’ all 17 corrections applied: 6 semantic (G15 LOUD not SILENT, F1 triple duplication, T16 line, I0 gap, EG1 file ref, totals), 6 line refs (server.py systematic drift В±4-14), 5 missing items.

summary: P6: Config & Policy SSOT вЂ” 19 sections, 7 findings, compliance matrix
details: Created P6_config_policy.md covering: config.json structure (278L, 17 sections), config_loader.py (7 functions, 3 defaults), consumption map (8 consumers), TF/Symbol/Polling/Redis/Preview/Calendar/Stitching/Limit/Anchor policies, config validation (_validate_config only in connector вЂ” gap), caching (UI hot-reload 0.5s, others read-once), client policy bridge (/api/config), 14 hardcoded policy audits (H1-H14), 7 client hardcodes (C1-C7), SSOT compliance matrix (8 SSOT / 3 duplicated / 4 mismatch). Self-review 6.5/10 в†’ corrections: _TF_CAP 4/8 values wrong (M15-H1=20000, H4=5000), _WARMUP_TF_PRIORITY order+members, _BACKOFF_CFG data structure, config.json systematic line offset (~12 refs), F4 elevated to 3-way mismatch (config 1800 / UDS 120 / worker default 60).
why: Code-first documentation of all config and policy for SSOT compliance audit
goal: Complete reference enabling config consolidation and identifying policy split-brain
risks: Config.json line numbers will drift with any config edit
notes: Critical finding: PREVIEW_CURR_TTL_S 3-way mismatch (config=1800, UDS=120, tick_preview default=60). build_uds_from_config never wires this param. TF_ALLOWLIST in buckets.py is hardcoded duplicate of config.json.

summary: P7: Gap Analysis вЂ” 47 findings, 6 cross-cutting patterns, remediation roadmap
details: Created P7_gap_analysis.md synthesizing ALL findings from P1-P6: 2 HIGH (tick drops SILENT, PREVIEW_TTL 3-way mismatch), 10 MEDIUM, 24 LOW, 11 INFO. 6 cross-cutting patterns identified: A-SSOT Duplication (8), B-Silent Drops (8+40 bare except), C-Final/Preview integrity (4), D-Config Distribution (6), E-Disk/Bootstrap boundary (3), F-Code Hygiene (3). Invariant coverage matrix shows I5 (degraded-but-loud) most violated. Rule compliance matrix. Full except Exception audit: 40 in production (26 bare/silent, 14 logged). Remediation roadmap: S1-S2 (HIGH priority), S3-S9 (MEDIUM, standalone slices), S10-S18 (LOW backlog). Self-review 5/10 -> corrections: 4 distribution table row errors, 41->40 count fix, 8 line reference corrections, P4-F2 moved from Pattern A to Pattern B, M-5 added to remediation as S6.5.
why: Final synthesis document enabling prioritized remediation of all system gaps
goal: Unified gap analysis as actionable remediation backlog with evidence
risks: Line numbers will drift; findings count may change as code evolves
notes: Series complete: 7 documents, ~7000 lines, 47 findings. Top priorities: S1 (wire preview_curr_ttl_s) and S2 (tick aggregator loud drops). Most violated invariant: I5 (degraded-but-loud). 0 TODO/FIXME in production code (positive).

summary: P8: Verification & Clarifications Pack вЂ” 10 questions, file:line evidence, GO/NO-GO verdict
details: Created P8_clarifications.md answering 10 critical verification questions (Q1-Q10) with file:line evidence. Classifications: Q1(C) Preview TTL 3-way mismatch (config=1800/UDS=120/worker=60, effective=1800 via passthrough), Q2(C) tick_agg drops SILENT (I5 violated), Q3(B) I4 wording needs update (dual plane routing by design), Q4(A) tick_promoted correctly in preview-only plane, Q5(C) two _choose_better_bar with different tiebreak (UDS returns existing, disk returns incoming), Q6(A) cold_load/disk_policy truth table correct, Q7(B) stitching active without market boundary guard (docs incomplete), Q8(C) default complete=True in 5 locations (I3 risk), Q9(C) no unified process watchdog/degraded[] pipeline (I5 violated), Q10(C) dual Redis key convention undocumented (UpdatesBus raw / Preview Ring symbol_key). GO/NO-GO: GO with conditions S1(wire TTL) + S2(tick drops loud) + doc updates. No ADR needed вЂ” no invariant changes required.
why: Verification layer before TO-BE remediation вЂ” validate P1-P7 findings against actual code with file:line proof
goal: All 10 questions answered with evidence, enabling confident transition to remediation patches
risks: Line numbers may drift if code edited. Evidence valid as of session date.
notes: Series status: P1-P7 (spec) + P8 (verification). Top priorities confirmed: S1 (wire preview_curr_ttl_s via build_uds_from_config) and S2 (tick_agg stats emission + periodic warning). Most violated invariant: I5 (degraded-but-loud). Dedup divergence (Q5) is LOW risk due to watermark protection. Stitching (Q7) is TV-like by design but needs calendar-awareness documentation.

summary: S1: Preview TTL SSOT - wire config -> UDS, eliminate 3-way mismatch
details: PATCH resolves P8-Q1 3-way mismatch (config=1800, UDS hardcode=120, worker fallback=60). Changes: (1) uds.py: renamed PREVIEW_CURR_TTL_S -> _DEFAULT_PREVIEW_CURR_TTL_S=1800, wired in build_uds_from_config via cfg.get(preview_curr_ttl_s). (2) tick_preview_worker.py: curr_ttl_s now Optional[int], passes None when config key absent -> UDS uses SSOT default. (3) 4 tests: config wiring, default fallback, publish with/without explicit TTL. Effective TTL path: config.json -> build_uds_from_config -> UDS ctor -> _preview_curr_ttl_s. engine_b.py:458 calls publish_preview_bar without ttl_s -> now gets 1800 from UDS ctor (was 120).
why: 3-way TTL mismatch violated SSOT invariant; config value 1800 was ignored by UDS hardcode 120
goal: Single source of truth for preview TTL: config.json -> UDS ctor, no hardcodes
risks: Existing preview bars in Redis may have old TTL until natural expiry
rollback_steps: Revert uds.py: restore PREVIEW_CURR_TTL_S=120, remove cfg.get wiring
notes: VERIFY: 4/4 tests PASS. Exit gates EG17(config_singleton)=OK. No regressions in 20 related tests.

summary: S2: Tick aggregator drops - degraded-but-loud WARNING emission
details: PATCH resolves P8-Q2 silent tick drops (I5 violated). Changes: (1) tick_preview_worker.py: added _last_drops_total field, extended _maybe_emit_stats() to merge tick_agg.stats() into TICK_PREVIEW_STATS payload, compute drops_total delta (dropped_late_bucket + dropped_closed_bucket + dropped_no_bucket + dropped_calendar), emit WARNING TICK_AGG_DROPS on positive delta. First emit silent (initializes baseline). Rate-limited by existing 60s stats interval. (2) 4 pytest tests: payload contains tick_agg_stats, no warning on first emit, warning on drops increase, no warning when stable. Uses caplog fixture (assertLogs incompatible with pytest).
why: Tick drops were completely silent - violated I5 degraded-but-loud invariant
goal: Any tick drops produce visible WARNING in logs with count+details
risks: First 60s window after start has no baseline (by design - avoids false alarm on startup priming)
notes: VERIFY: 4/4 tests PASS. ticks_rejected_tf excluded from drops_total (it is a filter, not a drop). Exit gates EG24(tick_preview_calendar)=OK.

summary: P8 doc updates: I4 dual-plane routing + stitching calendar-awareness warning
details: (1) P5 I4: Title changed from Odin update-potik dlya UI to Dual Plane Update Routing - reflects actual A->C->B design where /api/updates routes by TF: preview_tf_allowlist->Preview Ring, else->UpdatesBus. Bridge final->preview is best-effort. (2) P4 15.3: Added P8-Q7 limitation warning - stitching closes ALL gaps including weekend/session breaks/news gaps; no calendar-awareness guard exists; raw SSOT data needed for gap analysis (SMC FVG). (3) Mini-discovery: tick_promoted confirmed UI-plane only by design (publish_promoted_bar writes only to preview ring, not disk/commit_final_bar, not in FINAL_SOURCES). (4) Exit gates: 19/24 OK, 5 FAIL pre-existing (EG12 preview_not_on_disk, EG14 api_splitbrain, EG15 overlay_anchor_sentinel, EG16 htf_allowlist, EG20 gap_budget). All 6 target gates PASS: EG2/EG3/EG4/EG5/EG17/EG24.
why: Documentation must track actual invariant semantics; exit gates verify no regression from S1/S2
goal: Accurate docs + regression-free evidence for S1/S2 patches
risks: None - documentation only + verification
notes: tick_promoted: uds.py:675-721 publish_promoted_bar() -> _redis.publish_preview_event() only. No ADR needed.

details: 1) edgeProbe.ts: adaptive backoff (2s-5s-10s-20s-60s cap), single-inflight AbortController, visibility API (60s when hidden), rate-limited logging (first + every 10th). Kills 90% console spam. 2) diagSelectors.ts: removed PROBE_UNKNOWN/BACKEND_DOWN statuses, WS_UNAVAILABLE is always primary when ws!=open, edge probe shown as secondary detail string. No more status jumping. 3) StatusOverlay: show delay 1500ms + hide delay 2000ms (was immediate). lastMsg pattern prevents empty content during hide transition. 4) StatusBar: WS_UNAVAILABLE color added, BACKEND_DOWN removed.

risks: edgeProbe adaptive backoff means slower detection of recovery (up to 60s at cap vs 15s fixed). Mitigated by visibility API (immediate probe on tab focus).

notes: typecheck 0/0. Key fix: CONNECTING.critical = attempt > 0.
goal: Overlay appears once and stays rock-solid. Console: max 2 lines from our code, browser native WS/GET errors reduced to ~2/min at cap.
details: Root cause overlay flicker: CONNECTING (critical=false) fires on every reconnect attempt, cancelling show timer before 1500ms elapses. Fix: CONNECTING.critical = ws_reconnect_attempt > 0. Root cause console spam: WS maxReconnectMs=5s + edgeProbe backoff 2-60s = browser logs every failed GET and WebSocket. Fix: WS maxReconnectMs=30s, probe backoff 5s-15s-30s-60s-120s, hidden tab=120s. Both WS and probe log only first failure, then silence.
why: User reported overlay still flickering and console still flooded at attempt #22/#40.
summary: Kill console spam + fix overlay flicker: CONNECTING critical in loop, 30s WS cap, 120s probe cap, log-once
risks: 30s WS reconnect means slower recovery detection. Tab focus triggers immediate probe.

notes: typecheck 0/0. QUIET_AFTER=3, QUIET_INTERVAL_MS=60000.
goal: Console: 3 WS errors + 1 probe error in first ~10s, then 1 WS error per 60s. Zero probe errors after first. Tab focus = instant retry.
details: Root cause: browser natively logs every failed new WebSocket() and every non-2xx fetch(). Cannot suppress from JS. Fix: minimize network requests. WS: 3 fast attempts (2s/3s/4.5s) then quiet mode (60s). Tab focus triggers immediate retry. edgeProbe: probe-once-then-stop strategy. After first classify result, no more automatic probes. Re-probe only on tab focus or 'пїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅiпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅпїЅ' (probeNow()). close(): null all handlers + check readyState before ws.close() to avoid 'WebSocket closed before established' warning.
why: User saw continuous browser-native error spam (WebSocket failed + GET 500) that rate-limiting could not suppress.
summary: Quiet degraded mode: 3 fast WS attempts then 60s, probe-once strategy, safe close()
risks: 60s quiet mode means up to 60s detection lag when backend comes up. Tab focus mitigates for active users.

notes: 1-word fix. Redis db=1 namespace=v3_local has 237 snap keys. prime:ready=true with all 13 symbols.
goal: ws_server reads from Redis (primed by connector/m1_poller) instead of blocked disk path
details: ROOT CAUSE: ws_server _uds_read_window used ReadPolicy(disk_policy='bootstrap') with prefer_redis=False (default). Flow: RAM empty -> disk_allowed check -> BOOTSTRAP_WINDOW_S=60 expired -> DISK_HOTPATH_BLOCKED -> candles=0. Meanwhile connector/m1_poller had primed Redis with 237 keys (all 13 symbols x 8 TFs). FIX: prefer_redis=True enables Redis cold-load path. VERIFY: direct UDS test H1=100, H4=152, M1=500 bars from redis_tail. 7/7 WS tests pass.
why: candles=0 for ALL symbols/TFs after 60s bootstrap window. DISK_HOTPATH_BLOCKED logged 24+ times. Users see empty charts.
summary: P0 BLOCKER FIX: ws_server ReadPolicy prefer_redis=True (was False -> 0 candles forever)
risks: redis_small_tail/prime_incomplete warnings expected for some TFs (non-blocking degraded-but-loud)
rollback_steps: revert ws_server.py L244: ReadPolicy(disk_policy='bootstrap') without prefer_redis=True

notes: VERIFY: python syntax OK, 8 inline tests passed (valid/bad_t_ms/missing_field/h_lt_l/NaN/batch_drop/dup_detection/unsorted_detection). Does NOT drop for unsorted/dup - only warns. candle_map.py already validates input; this guard is defense-in-depth for output.
goal: Degraded-but-loud: catch bad candles before WS send, log+warn, drop corrupt data instead of sending it to UI
details: _guard_candle_shape(candle): validates single v4 Candle dict (t_ms>0, o/h/l/c numeric+finite, NaN check, h>=l, v optional numeric). Returns issue string or None. _guard_candles_output(candles, sym, tf, frame_type): batch guard, drops bad candles in-place (degraded-but-loud), checks t_ms monotonicity (duplicates + unsorted), returns warnings list. Wired into all 3 frame builders: _build_full_frame, _build_delta_frame, _build_scrollback_frame. Guard warnings merged with existing warnings in meta.warnings[]. ~100 LOC added.
why: H-3/S19 gap: WS had 0 output guards while HTTP server.py has 5. Any corrupt candle from UDS/candle_map would reach UI silently.
summary: T6/S19: WS output guards for candle shape + monotonicity on all outgoing frames
risks: Guard is conservative - only drops clearly invalid data (missing fields, NaN, h<l). Monotonicity is warn-only, not drop.

summary: Fix LWC Cannot update oldest data crash: head guard + rAF queue + backend sort
details: Race condition: preview bar for N+1 arrives before final bar for N. LWC series.update() crashes when bar.time < head time. Two bugs fixed: (1) _rafPending single-bar overwrite lost intermediate bars in delta frames вЂ” changed to _rafQueue accumulator with dedup-by-t_ms + sort + apply-in-order. (2) No head guard вЂ” added _headTMs tracking: bars with t_ms < head are skipped (degraded-but-loud console.warn). Backend defense-in-depth: candles sorted by t_ms in _delta_loop before sending. Reset logic added to setData/clearAll/destroy.
why: LWC Cannot update oldest data [object Object] crash in production. Root cause: previewв†’final race between m1_poller final commit and tick_preview_worker preview bars.
goal: Zero LWC crashes from out-of-order delta bars. Late final bars gracefully skipped (preview data already on chart is visually correct). All delta bars delivered to LWC (no more rAF single-bar loss).
risks: Late final bars are skipped (not applied to chart) вЂ” preview OHLCV is usually identical to final, so visual impact is negligible. If OHLCV differs, the chart shows preview values until next full frame.
notes: Pre-existing svelte-check error (FrameType vs config) unrelated. Finding 067 (event_ts end-excl) still open.

summary: Fix entry-071 regression: final bars never replace preview + DUP_T_MS spam
details: TWO bugs from entry 071 head guard: (1) _headTMs hard-skip blocks ALL final bars for completed minutes (preview N+1 advances head before final N arrives в†’ final N skipped forever). (2) DUP_T_MS fires at tick frequency because multiple preview events per delta poll. FIX BACKEND: dedup events by open_ms in _delta_loop before candle mapping вЂ” complete=true (final) wins over preview for same open_ms. FIX FRONTEND: replace _headTMs hard-skip with try/catch around series.update() + new _replacePastBars() method that does targeted data array splice with viewport preservation via getVisibleLogicalRange()/setVisibleLogicalRange(). Late bars (t_ms < head) are collected and batch-replaced via single setData() call per flush cycle.
why: Entry 071 head guard was too strict вЂ” skipped final bars arriving after preview of next minute, breaking the I3 final>preview invariant for ui_v4
goal: Final bars overwrite preview bars instantly; DUP_T_MS eliminated; no LWC crash
risks: setData() for late bars may cause brief flicker (once per minute max); viewport preservation relies on getVisibleLogicalRange() accuracy
notes: Reverts the problematic parts of 071 while keeping the beneficial _rafQueue accumulator pattern. Backend dedup ensures I3 (final>preview) at the wire level.

summary: Boundary-tolerant derive: fix missing derived bars at session open/close boundaries
details: ROOT CAUSE: FXCM broker often omits the first M1 bar after market open/daily break reopen (e.g., M1 23:00 on Sunday / daily break reopen). derive_bar() required ALL trading slots present (has_range strict check), so M3/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4 23:00 were all missing -- cascading through the entire chain. FIX: Added _collect_boundary_tolerant() helper in core/derive.py. When strict has_range() fails and is_trading_fn is provided, falls back to boundary-tolerant collection. A missing bar is tolerated if it's at a session boundary: is_trading_fn(t-step)=False (session open) or is_trading_fn(t+step)=False (session close). Gaps in the middle of a session still correctly fail. Partial bars get extensions: boundary_partial=True, source_count, expected_count (degraded-but-loud per rule 9). Backfill regenerated 1457 new bars across all symbols (0 errors). Affects both live DeriveEngine and backfill_cascade tool (both use derive_bar from core/derive.py).
why: All derived TFs (M3-H4) were missing their first bar after every session reopen (Sunday open + weekday daily break), causing visible gaps in UI charts. TradingView handles this correctly by deriving partial bars.
goal: Derived bars exist at every session boundary, matching TradingView behavior. No data loss at market open/reopen.
risks: Low. Boundary-partial bars have 1 fewer source bar (e.g., 2/3 for M3, 4/5 for M5). OHLCV accuracy: Open price starts from second bar of session instead of first -- acceptable since broker didn't provide first bar data. Extension metadata makes it auditable.

notes: H1 19:00 on Friday remains missing -- separate issue: M1 19:00 missing from broker data mid-session (not a boundary gap). Needs data repair. fx_24x5_utc_winter group (GBP_CAD etc) not affected -- broker provides M1 at break reopen for those.
disk_layer.py _read_jsonl_filtered line 66: 'open_ms > to_open_ms: continue' = inclusive. Not changing server semantics вЂ” UI defense-in-depth is correct layer.
goal: Scrollback loads history without crash, no infinite loop on setData failure
details: Root cause: server to_open_ms is inclusive (<=) in _read_jsonl_filtered вЂ” scrollback returns bars already on chart. prependData() passed duplicate timestamps to series.setData() causing LWC Candlestick renderer crash. Three defenses: (1) dedup вЂ” build Set of new bar times, filter existing data to exclude overlaps; (2) WhitespaceData guard вЂ” filter series.data() items without OHLC fields; (3) move state reset (idle/isFetching) AFTER successful setData + try/catch to prevent 356-error/sec infinite loop. Same guards added to _replacePastBars for late-final path.
why: UI crash: scrolling left triggers 'Value is null' x356/sec, chart disappears, UI freezes completely. Page refresh only temp fix.
summary: Fix LWC 'Value is null' crash on scrollback: dedup + WhitespaceData guard + crash-loop prevention
risks: Dedup silently drops overlap bar вЂ” acceptable since same bar exists in both sets. WhitespaceData filter could reduce displayed bars if LWC internal state corrupted вЂ” user sees slightly fewer bars vs crash.

summary: AUDIT: Derive engine session alignment confirmed TV-correct (modulo bucketing). No code changes needed.
details: Full audit of derive engine alignment: (1) bucket_start_ms() in core/buckets.py uses floor-division modulo math ((ts-anchor)//tf*tf+anchor) вЂ” identical to TV formula BucketTime=CandleTime-(CandleTime mod TF). (2) derive_triggers() in core/derive.py uses _bucket_start_ms for all trigger calculations. (3) TickAggregator in tick_agg.py uses bucket_start_ms for preview bars. (4) assert_invariants() in bars.py validates (open_time_ms % tf_ms == 0) as runtime guard. (5) FXCM provider applies assert_invariants on every row вЂ” non-aligned bars rejected. (6) _collect_boundary_tolerant() handles missing M1 at session open/close (boundary_partial=True marker). Real data verification: NAS100 daily break 21:59в†’23:00 вЂ” first M3 is 23:00 (mod 180000=0); XAU/USD session open вЂ” all M3/M5/H4 correctly grid-aligned; H4 anchor 82800s=23:00 UTC produces correct 23/03/07/11/15/19 grid. CONCLUSION: System already implements session alignment. No data-driven alignment issue exists.
why: User concern about data-driven vs session-aligned bucket formation (first bar at 23:01 creating misaligned M3). Needed formal proof that derive engine uses modulo math, not data-driven timestamps.
goal: Formal proof that derive engine alignment matches TradingView session alignment. Zero code changes вЂ” documentation only.
risks: None вЂ” read-only audit, no code changes.
notes: Evidence: bucket_start_ms L34, derive.py L486, tick_agg.py L94, bars.py L59-63, provider.py L244. Data: NAS100 tf_180 2026-02-19 at 23:00=mod0, XAU_USD tf_300 2026-02-23 at 00:00=mod0, XAU_USD tf_14400 at 03:00=anchor-correct.

summary: Slice 1+2: flat bar display filter (D1 weekend artifacts) + rebuild_from_m1 tool (8751 bars filled across 13 symbols M3-H4)
details: SLICE 1 вЂ” Flat bar display filter: (a) candle_map.py: added _is_display_flat_bar() вЂ” checks extensions.calendar_pause_flat OR (O==H==L==C && v<=4.0). map_bar_to_candle_v4() returns None for flat bars (WS path). (b) server.py: added same filter in _normalize_bar_window_v1() after OHLCV extraction (HTTP path). _FLAT_BAR_MAX_VOLUME=4.0 constant. Both paths: flat bars silently dropped from display (data preserved on disk). SLICE 2 вЂ” rebuild_from_m1.py tool: staged cascade approach: Stage 1 reads ALL M1 from disk в†’ derives M3 + M5 via GenericBuffer + derive_bar (calendar-aware). Stage 2-5: each reads source TF from disk (including just-written bars) в†’ derives next TF (M5в†’M15в†’M30в†’H1в†’H4). Uses _has_on_disk() dedup to skip existing bars. CLI: python -m tools.rebuild_from_m1 [--dry-run] [--symbol X] [--start/--end]. Full run results: XAU/USD=825, EUSTX50=250, GBP/CAD=901, GER30=305, HKG33=4, NAS100=796, NGAS=646, NZD/CAD=874, SPX500=796, US30=813, USD/CAD=850, USD/JPY=873, XAG/USD=818. Total=8751 new bars. Mostly M5 (from M1), cascading to M15/M30/H1 where needed.
why: User screenshots showed: (1) historical gaps on M30/H4 across all symbols вЂ” derive fix (Entry 075) was forward-only, existing JSONL still had holes. (2) Flat bars visible on D1 вЂ” Saturday weekend artifacts from FXCM (O==H==L==C, v<=4) passed through without filtering.
goal: Fill all historical gaps in derived TFs (M3-H4) from existing M1 data + remove visual flat bar noise on D1 charts.
risks: Flat bar filter uses volume<=4.0 threshold вЂ” legitimate low-volume bars with O==H==L==C could theoretically be filtered. Mitigated: only FXCM weekend bars match this pattern (verified on XAU/USD D1: only 4 bars, all Saturdays). Rebuild tool writes derived bars вЂ” if M1 data has errors, derived bars inherit them.
rollback_steps: Slice 1: revert candle_map.py and server.py flat bar filter lines
Slice 2: delete newly written bars from tf_300/tf_180/tf_900/tf_1800/tf_3600 JSONL files (bars with src=derived written after 2026-02-23T14:44)
notes: Exit code 1 from rebuild is PowerShell stderr capture of 'redis unavailable' warning (non-fatal). HKG33 had only 4 new bars вЂ” its M1в†’M5 coverage was already good. Cascade effectiveness: most fills at M5 level, with 1-3 bars cascading up to M15/M30/H1 per symbol.

summary: Slice 3+4+5: Live preview candles for ALL TFs (M1-H4) + HUD streaming price + D1 alignment verified
details: SLICE 3 вЂ” Live candles all TFs: (a) config.json: preview_tick_tfs_s expanded from [60,180] to [60,180,300,900,1800,3600,14400]. All 4 gates (tick_preview_worker, UDS allowlist, WS preview_set, commit_final_bar bridge) are config-driven and open automatically. (b) tick_preview_worker.py: added anchor_offset_ms parameter, reads day_anchor_offset_s from config (82800), passes to TickAggregator. Critical for H4: anchor=82800000 gives correct 23:00-UTC grid (proven: bucket at 23:00 vs wrong 00:00 with anchor=0). For M1-H1: 82800 is a multiple of all TFs(60-3600), so anchor makes no difference. (c) TickAggregator is fully generic вЂ” supports any TF. M3 still derived from M1 via _M1toM3Buffer (not from ticks). M5-H4 aggregated directly from ticks with correct bucket alignment. SLICE 4 вЂ” HUD streaming price: auto-fixed by Slice 3. Reactive chain already complete: tick в†’ TickAgg в†’ UDS publish_preview в†’ Redis в†’ WS delta_loop(1s) в†’ frameRouter в†’ currentFrame store в†’ lastPrice=last.c в†’ ChartHud. With all TFs in preview set, HUD price updates ~1/s for any TF. SLICE 5 вЂ” D1 alignment: verified broker-determined timestamps (22:00 UTC = FXCM winter/EST). Matches TradingView convention. No code changes needed. D1 excluded from preview (86400 not in list) вЂ” broker-direct, different anchor, 24h restart risk.
why: User wants TradingView-class UX: live growing candles on all TFs (M5/H4 were static between bar closes), streaming HUD price, D1 correctness.
goal: All TFs from M1 to H4 show live-growing preview candles. HUD price updates reactively every ~1s. D1 stays broker-direct (final only).
risks: (1) H4 preview loses state on tick_preview_worker restart (up to 4h of data). Mitigated: final derived H4 replaces preview at bucket close. (2) Redis load: 13 symbols x 7 TFs = 91 preview keys updating every 250ms = ~364 writes/s max. Redis handles this easily. (3) Promoted bars for M5+ (tick_promoted) give instant mock-final at rollover; real derived final arrives 1-10s later via commit_final_bar bridge.
notes: anchor_offset_ms proof: H4 with anchor=82800000 -> bucket at 23:00 UTC (correct); with anchor=0 -> bucket at 00:00 UTC (wrong 1h shift). M5 with both anchors: identical (82800 mod 300 = 0). D1 bars verified: 2026-02-19 22:00 UTC O=4995.89 (winter EST=22:00). Weekend flat bar 2026-02-21 v=3 now hidden by Slice 1 filter.

summary: ADR-0004: mid-session gap tolerance in derive cascade + full 13-symbol audit + NGAS H4 fix
details: DISCOVERY: Full audit of 13 symbols x 8 TF. Found: (1) NGAS H4 stuck at 2026-02-20 due to M1 mid-session gaps from illiquid broker data. (2) GER30 pre-market 472 M1 bars (real FXCM data, not bug). (3) US30 136 Saturday bars (low-vol FXCM data). (4) fx_24x5 break boundary noise 130-224 bars each. (5) HKG33 Feb 17-19 gap = Chinese New Year (legitimate). PATCH: Added MAX_MID_SESSION_GAPS=3 constant and mid-session tolerance to _collect_boundary_tolerant() in core/derive.py. derive_bar() now uses tolerant fallback with max_mid_session_gaps=3. Degraded bars marked with extension mid_session_gaps=N (I5 degraded-but-loud). Strict path unchanged (no extensions when all bars present). Rebuilt NGAS: 6 new H4 bars (Feb 22-23), 13 new H1, total 453 new bars across all TFs. VERIFY: 46 existing tests pass, 5 targeted mid-session tolerance tests pass. Entry 075 verified intact. Calendar violations analysis: only FXCM boundary data, no code bugs.
why: NGAS (illiquid instrument) had H4 derivation stuck because FXCM does not provide M1 bars for minutes with zero trades. Single missing M1 mid-session cascaded to M5->M15->M30->H1->H4 failure.
goal: Fix NGAS H4 stuck at Feb 20, allow resilient derivation for illiquid instruments while maintaining degraded-but-loud invariant.
risks: Derived bars with mid_session_gaps may have slightly inaccurate OHLCV (missing minute could have had extreme). Risk is minimal for V=0 missing minutes. Rollback: set MAX_MID_SESSION_GAPS=0.
rollback_steps: Purge bars with mid_session_gaps extension from NGAS SSOT files
notes: ADR-0004 written to docs/ADR-0004-mid-session-gap-tolerance.md. Budget: 3 gaps per bucket. Backwards compatible (default=0 in function, constant=3 used by derive_bar). Calendar violations (GER30/US30/fx_24x5 boundary bars) left as-is per user decision.

H4 is DERIVED ONLY per ADR-0002. FXCM H4 uses anchor=22:00 UTC which doesn't match our day_anchor_offset_s config. fetch_tf_backfill should never be used with --tf 14400. D1 checked separately: all src=history with hours 21/22 UTC (DST), no action needed (D1 not derived yet).
goal: Clean H4 data: only derived bars with correct TV anchor grid in tf_14400.
details: PROBLEM: After massive backfill (entry 083), H4 chart showed double bars. Root cause: fetch_tf_backfill wrote H4 bars directly from FXCM API with anchor=22:00 UTC (hours 2,6,10,14,18,22). Our system derives H4 from H1 with TV anchor (hours 3,7,11,15,19,23 UTC based on day_anchor_offset_s). Two incompatible anchor grids coexisted in tf_14400/, doubling bar count. AUDIT: 11 of 13 symbols had MIXED data (history+derived). Only USD_JPY and XAG_USD were clean (derived only). Example XAU_USD: 6282 total = 3672 history (FXCM anchor) + 2610 derived (TV anchor). PATCH: Removed all src=history bars from tf_14400 for all 13 symbols. ~38K FXCM bars removed. After cleanup: 2083-2614 derived bars per symbol. VERIFY: All 13 symbols now contain only src=derived bars in tf_14400.
why: H4 chart displayed double the expected bars due to two incompatible anchor grids (FXCM 22:00 vs TV 23:00) mixed in same tf_14400 directory.
summary: H4 cleanup: removed all src=history FXCM bars from tf_14400 (wrong anchor grid), kept only derived
risks: H4 bar count reduced (removed FXCM history). Some date ranges may have fewer H4 bars if derive chain didn't cover them. Server restart needed to refresh Redis/RAM.
rollback_steps: Re-run fetch_tf_backfill --tf 14400 to re-fetch FXCM H4 (not recommended - anchor mismatch)
Or rebuild_from_m1 to regenerate derived H4 from existing H1

notes: VERIFY: python -m tools.fetch_tf_backfill --tf 14400 --n 100 --all -> ERROR exit code 1.
goal: Fail-fast guard preventing FXCM H4 fetch that produces anchor-mismatched bars.
details: Added DERIVED_ONLY_TFS guard to fetch_tf_backfill.py. TF=14400 (H4) is blocked by default because FXCM H4 has anchor=22:00 UTC which mismatches our TV anchor (day_anchor_offset_s). H4 must be built via DeriveEngine (rebuild_from_m1). Guard shows clear error message and exits with code 1. Override available via --force-derived-tf flag for edge cases.
why: Prevent repeat of entry 083 issue where FXCM H4 bars (wrong anchor grid) were fetched and mixed with derived H4 bars, causing double bars on chart.
summary: Guard: fetch_tf_backfill blocks --tf 14400 (derived-only TF, ADR-0002)
risks: None. Guard only blocks --tf 14400, all other TFs unaffected. --force-derived-tf available as escape hatch.

FIX 1: candle_map.py + server.py flat bar filter now skips HTF (tf_s >= 14400). FIX 2: ws_server disk_policy changed from bootstrap to explicit (ws is separate process, RAM/Redis can be stale). Verified: WS D1 now shows 1000 candles including Feb 23 bar. Both UIs show same last bar.

summary: D1 appear-then-disappear fix: seq passthrough + forward-gap HTF + switch race elimination
details: AUDIT: D1 bars appear then disappear on TF switch (cache restored -> background loadBarsFull replaces). 4 DEFECTS found: (1) seq dropped in _normalize_update_events_window_v1 L877 - client cannot dedup events, (2) FORWARD_GAP_MAX_BARS=3 too tight for D1 weekend/holiday gaps, (3) TF/symbol switch always calls loadBarsFull on cache hit causing setBars overwrite race, (4) boot_id mismatch triggers full reload flash. FIXES: (1) Added seq passthrough in server.py _normalize_update_events_window_v1. (2) Replaced constant FORWARD_GAP_MAX_BARS=3 with getForwardGapMaxBars(tf): D1=10, H4=15, H1=8, default=3. (3) Removed background loadBarsFull on cache hit in both elSymbol.change and elTf.change handlers - polling /api/updates + fetchNewerBarsFromDisk will handle incremental updates. (4) Added console.warn for boot_id change for diagnostics. VERIFY: JS syntax OK, Python syntax OK, 4 unit tests pass, seq=42 passthrough confirmed, D1 flat bar filter HTF skip confirmed.
why: D1 last bar appeared then disappeared on TF switch. Subsequent switches disappeared faster (stale cache). Users frustrated by cyclical D1 issues.
goal: Stable D1 display without bar disappearance on TF/symbol switch
risks: Removing background loadBarsFull on cache hit means cache staleness relies on polling. For D1 (1 event/day) this is fine. For LTF, polling interval is short enough.
rollback_steps: Revert server.py: remove seq passthrough in _normalize_update_events_window_v1
Revert app.js: restore loadBarsFull().catch(() => { }) in both elSymbol.change and elTf.change else branches
notes: VERIFY: (1) python -c compile server.py OK, (2) node --check app.js OK, (3) pytest test_public_api_window_v1.py 4/4 pass, (4) inline test seq=42 passthrough OK, (5) D1 flat bar kept, M5 flat bar dropped. Entry continues from 086 (flat bar HTF exemption).

summary: D1 weekend artifact fix: weekend-aware flat filter + connector unclosed/weekend guards + disk cleanup 24 bars
details: ROOT CAUSE: Entry 086 disabled HTF flat bar filter (tf_s>=14400) to preserve just-opened bars. Side effect: FXCM weekend artifacts (Sat flat O==H==L==C V=1-5) and unclosed Monday bars (cold_start commits forming bar as final) passed through to UI. Audit: 13/13 symbols had Saturday flat bars, 5/13 had Sunday flat bars (indices), all had unclosed Monday bars. THREE-LAYER FIX: (1) Display filter (server.py + candle_map.py): HTF flat bar filter now weekend-aware: LTF filters all flat, HTF filters flat ONLY if open_time weekday is Friday(4) or Saturday(5) UTC. Just-opened bars (Mon-Thu) preserved. (2) Connector guard (engine_b.py): _cold_start_base_from_broker skips unclosed bars (close_ms > now_ms) and weekend artifact D1+ bars (weekday 4/5). _fetch_base_from_broker_on_close also skips weekend D1+ bars. (3) Disk cleanup: tools/cleanup_d1_weekend.py removed 24 flat weekend + unclosed bars across 12 symbols.
why: User report: D1 shows flat bar for yesterday after TF switch, persists and never updates. FXCM weekend artifacts + unclosed forming bars committed as final.
goal: D1 chart without weekend artifact flat bars; connector does not commit unclosed/weekend bars
risks: Real flat bars on Friday evening (unlikely for D1) would be filtered. Acceptable: D1 V<=4 on Friday = no actual trading.
rollback_steps: Revert server.py flat filter (remove weekday check, restore simple tf_s >= 14400 skip)
Revert candle_map.py (remove _is_weekend_open_utc, restore simple tf_s >= 14400 skip)
Revert engine_b.py (remove unclosed + weekend guards from _cold_start and _fetch_base)
Re-run connector cold_start to re-fetch weekend bars from FXCM
notes: VERIFY: (1) py_compile all 3 files OK. (2) Dry-run cleanup: 24 bars identified. (3) Cleanup executed: 24 bars removed across 12 symbols. (4) XAU_USD last D1: Sun Feb 22 real V=4494 (correct). Saturday flat + Monday unclosed gone. Weekend bars for indices (Sun open, wd=6) not targeted - will be addressed by calendar if needed.

summary: ADR-0012 P3: D1 live forming candle via tick relay in ws_server delta_loop
details: New flow in _delta_loop: when D1 TF has no update events, poll tick:last:{symbol} from Redis -> synthesize forming candle (complete=false, src=tick_relay) -> send via standard delta frame. WsSession._forming_ohlc tracks OHLC state. Reset on final bar arrival. Config: d1_live_tick_relay_enabled=true, d1_live_tick_relay_tfs_s=[86400]. Separate Redis client created in build_app via resolve_redis_spec. I4 not violated: tick relay = enrichment within single delta channel. VERIFY: py_compile OK, imports OK.

goal: Live D1 candle updating with ticks + fresh HUD price
risks: Additional Redis GET per delta poll (1/2s) пїЅ negligible. Forming candle has V=0 (synthetic).
rollback_steps: Restart ws_server пїЅ tick relay code guarded by flag
notes: I8: forming NOT written to disk/UDS. Final always replaces forming.
Rev2 replaces rev1. All 10/10 self-checks pass.

summary: ADR-0011: SSOT Broadcast Serialization (Global delta loop)
details: Replaced per-session delta_loop with a single _global_delta_loop. It groups sessions by (symbol, tf), fetches UDS updates once per target, serializes a single JSON payload, and uses safe fanout broadcast via asyncio.gather. Also ported ADR-0012 P3 tick relay logic into the global loop correctly.
why: Avoid CPU O(N) dict formation and serialization for 50+ clients watching the same currency chart, drastically decreasing server latency and stopping Event Loop starvation.
goal: Scale websocket streaming to handle heavily connected sessions cleanly.
risks: None, slow connections trigger warning and get pruned rather than causing backpressure.

summary: Fix HUD/Chart TF split-brain & cross-pollution
details:
1. Fixed frame variable leak in ws_server.py _global_delta_loop that was broadcasting 1D tick-relay frames to 1M clients if events were empty.
2. Added currentPair SSOT to frameRouter.ts.
3. Added schema_mismatch split-brain guard to reject delta frames that dont match currentPair.
4. App.svelte now reads HUD symbol/TF exclusively from currentPair, preventing flicker and desync.
why: User reported HUD showing 1D while chart stayed on 1M/5M.
goal: Atomicity between HUD TF and Chart TF.
risks: None. HUD is now strictly locked to the SSOT.

goal: Ensure continuous horizontal rendering of D1 charts.

summary: Server-side candle sanitization and deduplication
details: Added tail normalization (h >= max(ohlc), l <= min(ohlc)), negative volume stripping (v < 0 -> 0.0), and chronological deduplication (latest t_ms wins) for batch conversions.
why: Invalid backend artifacts could crash lightweight charts or cause graphical glitches.
goal: Ensure robust candle data parsing at the server boundary.
risks: None.

Backups in data_v3/XAU_USD/tf_*/backup_before_rebuild/
goal: TradingView-quality candles from M1 SSOT
details: ROOT CAUSE: FXCM pre-aggregated bars (src=history) in M5 (93.4%) and H1 (66.5%) had incorrect wicks vs M1 source. Proven: H1 bar diff up to 8 USD. Also M3 stale: 90% of bars were derived from since-updated M1. Tool cascade-derives M3+M5 from M1, M15 from M5, M30 from M15, H1 from M30, H4 from H1. Merge strategy: use derived where M1 exists, keep old FXCM bars pre-2025-10. Result: M3=37247 wicks fixed, M5=334, M15=201, M30=111, H1=54, H4=231. Verification: M5=100% match, H1=99.8%.
why: FXCM History API returns different aggregation for HTF than its own M1 data. M1 bars re-fetched but M3 never re-derived.
summary: Rebuild XAU/USD derived TFs (M3-H4) from M1 SSOT - fix broken FXCM wicks
risks: Old bars (pre-M1 era) kept as-is; 4 cascade precision losses at H1 (max 26.88)
rollback_steps: Restore from _backup_before_rebuild in each tf_* dir

why: After restart mid-session, D1 candle showed wrong open (first tick price instead of session open)
summary: Fix D1 forming candle restart: seed from UDS instead of first tick

summary: ADR-0024 rev 2.0: FAQ, 5 new algorithms, Trader Experience Architecture, Growth Trajectory
details: Major expansion of ADR-0024. NEW SECTIONS: (1) В§2A Fundamental Architecture Decisions - explicit FAQ with reasoning for real-time/batch hybrid, UDS API data source, in-process architecture, 10-algorithm echelon plan, indispensability factors. (2) В§4.6-4.10: Premium/Discount zones, Inducement detection, Session/Killzone awareness, Confluence POI Engine (11-point scoring, A+/A/B/C grades), Zone Quality Model (aging/freshness/decay). (3) В§9A Trader Experience Architecture: 3 value levels (Automation/Intelligence/Education), strong trader workflow use case, Narrative Engine, Dashboard symbol prioritization, Replay 3 modes (Blind/Annotated/Mentor). (4) В§9B Growth Trajectory: 4-phase roadmap from Phase 1 MVP to Phase 4 Platform Scale with competitive position matrix. UPDATED: types.py (premium/discount + inducement + POI grades), config.json:smc (sessions, confluence, quality sections), P-slices (P5a-P5e for E2/E3 algorithms), Section 16 open questions (8 items). ADR grew from 1068 to ~1700 lines.
why: User review identified 5 fundamental gaps: no explicit FAQ, missing algorithms (Premium/Discount, Inducement, Sessions, Confluence, Quality), weak trader experience narrative, no growth roadmap, insufficient depth for TV-level competition
goal: Production-grade ADR that answers all fundamental questions with deep reasoning, positions system as indispensable SMC companion tool
risks: ADR is large (~1700 lines) вЂ” may need split into sub-documents per topic in future
notes: Applied Bug Hunter (skeptic: are all claims provable?) + Patch Master (quality: is every section actionable?) roles. Competitive moat: Confluence POI scoring + Narrative engine + Replay training = features no competitor has.

summary: D1 near-duplicate fix: 13 history@21:00 bars removed + _ensure_sorted_dedup near-dedup guard (tf_ms//12 threshold)
details: Root cause: XAU/USD D1 had 13 near-duplicate bar pairs (Oct 14-30 2025). ADR-0023 derive engine produces bars at 22:00 UTC (79200s anchor). Old FXCM history bars at 21:00 UTC (summer DST anchor=75600s) remained on disk. Different open_time_ms (+3600000ms) bypassed both watermark dedup and exact _ensure_sorted_dedup. P1-DATA: removed 13 history@21:00 bars from XAU_USD/tf_86400 (10017в†’10004 bars). P2-GUARD: _ensure_sorted_dedup now accepts tf_ms param; for D1 (tf_ms>=86400000) bars closer than tf_ms//12 (2h) are merged via _choose_better_bar. Applied in all 4 UDS read paths (RAM, preview, disk, Redis). P3-TEST: 7 test cases covering near-dedup scenarios. Cleanup: 4 temp diag scripts removed.
why: User reported duplicate D1 candles on XAU/USD chart (Oct 15-31). DST anchor jitter (21:00 vs 22:00) created invisible near-duplicates that passed all existing dedup guards.
goal: Zero near-duplicate bars on UI, runtime guard prevents recurrence during future DST transitions
risks: Near-dedup threshold (2h) is conservative; no legitimate D1 bars should be <2h apart. Guard only active for D1 (tf_ms>=86400000).
rollback_steps: Revert uds.py: remove tf_ms param from _ensure_sorted_dedup and 4 caller sites
notes: Other 12 symbols unaffected (no derived D1 overlap period). NAS100/H4 has 1 exact dupe (separate minor fix). pytest 227 pass, exit-gates 5/5 PASS.

summary: Client-side replay: TradingView-style candle-by-candle replay in UI v4
details: ADR-0027: Replay purely in client. replayStore.svelte.ts (Svelte 5 runes) manages cursor/play/speed. ReplayBar.svelte provides scrubber+play/pause+speed+step+exit controls. ChartPane intercept: replay active -> block deltas, slice candles at cursor, efficient update() for forward steps. SMC overlay filtered by cursor timestamp. TF switch during replay preserves cursor position via binary search. Keyboard: Space=play/pause, arrows=step, Shift+arrows=step*10, Esc=exit.

summary: Zone quality Horizon 1: Q1 CHoCH boost, Q2 TF-aware decay, Q3 FVG HTF relax, Q4 OB body-only
details: 4 quick wins for zone visual quality. Q4: OB zone=body (open-close) not wicks (high-low) -> tighter zones, fewer false mitigations. Q1: CHoCH OB gets 1.5x strength boost over BOS OB -> reversal zones more prominent. Q2: TF-aware decay profiles (H4: 4x slower, D1: 10x slower, expire 2000/5000 bars) -> HTF zones live weeks/months. Q3: FVG min_gap_atr_mult halved for H1+ (0.3->0.15 effective) -> more FVG on HTF where they matter most.
why: Replay testing showed: too many minor OB, zones too narrow (wicks), H4/D1 zones decay too fast, zero FVG on HTF. Trader sees soup instead of clean model.
goal: Fewer but higher quality zones; HTF zones persistent; FVG visible on H1+
risks: OB body-only may miss some valid wicks-based zones. FVG threshold relaxation may show noise FVG on volatile sessions.
rollback_steps: Or revert Q4: high=ob_bar.h, low=ob_bar.low in order_blocks.py

summary: ADR-0029 ER-10: F3 htf_align over-restrictive status filter — only accepted active, rejected partially_filled/tested
details: F3 _check_htf_alignment required status==active but real HTF zones quickly become partially_filled (FVG) or tested (OB). context_stack.py and engine.py mitigation both treat these as alive. Fix: _HTF_ALIVE_STATUSES = frozenset((active, tested, partially_filled)). Expected impact: +2 pts for OBs aligned with HTF zones.
why: F3 htf_align NEVER fires for real zones because HTF zones are rarely status=active — they get tested/partially_filled after first price interaction.
goal: F3 awards +2 when OB mid-price inside alive HTF zone regardless of tested/partially_filled status.
risks: Some zones that were C may become B. Grade distribution shifts upward. Tuning via config if needed.

details: _build_full_frame extracted zones/swings/levels/zone_grades from smc_wire but never included trend_bias. UI received None for all TFs. Root cause: SmcSnapshot.to_wire() correctly serializes trend_bias, but _build_full_frame frame dict omitted it. Fix: add trend_bias key to frame dict from smc_wire.get('trend_bias'). 1 LOC.
why: R_TRADER audit: trend_bias=None on ALL TFs despite engine computing correct values (H4=bullish, H1=bearish, M15=bullish). Wire payload simply never included the field.
goal: UI receives actual trend_bias from SMC engine for all TFs.
risks: None — additive field, backward compatible. UI already handles trend_bias in RenderFrame type.
notes: 424 pass, 7 pre-existing fail. No regressions. Requires ws_server restart.
rollback_steps: Remove trend_bias line from _build_full_frame frame dict in runtime/ws/ws_server.py

summary: PATCH B: F2 fvg_after — add price overlap alternative to time-only adjacency
details: F2 _check_fvg_after previously only checked time adjacency (3-bar window after OB anchor). Real trading requires FVG to PRICE-OVERLAP with OB, not just appear nearby in time. Fix: keep original time check (variant A) + add price overlap check (variant B: FVG range intersects OB range, FVG anchor after OB anchor). Either variant → +2. Expected: OBs with overlapping FVGs now score higher.
why: R_TRADER audit T1: OB bear grade=B(4/11) should be A — F2 fvg_after never fired because FVG anchor was outside 3-bar window despite clear price overlap with OB zone.
goal: F2 rewards OBs confirmed by FVG price overlap, not just temporal coincidence.
risks: Grade distribution shifts upward for OBs near FVGs. Tuning via config if needed.
notes: 424 pass, 7 pre-existing fail. Requires ws_server restart.
rollback_steps: Revert _check_fvg_after in core/smc/confluence.py to time-only check

summary: PATCH C: TF-adaptive SMC config via tf_overrides — M15 swing_period=3, lower OB/FVG thresholds
details: Root cause of cross-TF convergence: single global swing_period=5 produces zero M15-native zones, so get_display_snapshot fills M15 display entirely with H1/H4 injections. Fix: (1) config.json tf_overrides block per compute TF, (2) SmcConfig.for_tf(tf_s) merges base+override, (3) _compute_snapshot uses cfg=self._config.for_tf(tf_s). M15: swing_period=3, ob.min_impulse_atr_mult=1.0, fvg.min_gap_atr_mult=0.15. H1/H4 unchanged. S5 compliant.
why: R_TRADER audit: M15=M30=H1=H4 identical data. Trader cannot do top-down analysis. M15 native zones 0 → no LTF drill-down.
goal: M15 produces its own native zones; each viewer TF shows distinct SMC picture.
risks: M15 may produce many more zones with lower thresholds. Display budget + proximity filter mitigates. Tunable via config.
notes: 424 pass, 7 pre-existing. for_tf() verified: M15 gets overrides, H1 returns self unchanged.
rollback_steps: Revert cfg=self._config.for_tf(tf_s) to self._config in engine.py

summary: ADR-0031 audit fixes: remove dead 'neutral' bias, has_changes safety rail, ADR text corrections
details: R_BUG_HUNTER audit of ADR-0031 found 6 findings (F1-F6). Code fixes: (F1) removed 'neutral' from trend_bias type — detect_structure_events() never generates it, dead code path. (F2) SmcDelta.has_changes now includes 'or self.trend_bias is not None' — prevents edge case where pure bias change without structural elements is silently dropped. ADR text fixes: (F3) _handle_switch_pending → _handle_switch, (F4) thread-safety rationale corrected to single-threaded event loop + GIL, (F5) stale window honestly documented as potentially hours not minutes, (F6) inline snippet replaced with SmcRunner.get_bias_map() encapsulated call. Also: alignment indicator definition clarified (unanimous = all non-null TFs same).
why: ADR-0031 preflight audit before implementation — 0 blockers but 6 accuracy/safety corrections needed.
goal: Clean, accurate ADR-0031 ready for P1-P3 implementation.
risks: None — F2 code fix additive (1 LOC boolean guard), backward compatible. 65 tests pass.
notes: 65 pass, 0 fail on SMC test suite.
rollback_steps: Revert types.py: remove 'or self.trend_bias is not None' from has_changes, restore 'neutral' in comment

summary: R_TRADER v1.1 + R_CHART_UX v1.1: розширення ролей п'ятьма ICT-концептами — Fractals, Sessions/Killzones, IOFED, Momentum/Displacement, Context Flow
details: Trader: §1.3 (5 підсекцій): фрактали (Williams strict + nested + break), сесійні зняття (Asia/London/NY H/L + killzones), IOFED (5-step entry drill), momentum/displacement (candle quality + momentum shift), Context Flow (Wyckoff phases + MTF narrative). Оновлено Setup Eval (+IOFED/session/momentum checks), Grade Check (+4 нових питання), Chart Audit (+session levels/fractals/killzone). +12 glossary terms. Chart UX: Appendix A (5 visual specs для fractals/sessions/killzones/displacement/IOFED), оновлена ієрархія ваги (9→14 рівнів), render pipeline (7→16 кроків), overlay architecture (+HUD layer), scope table (+6 нових елементів), animation rules (+6), typography (+7).
why: Трейдер і Chart UX мали бути безпрограшними спеціалістами на рівні institutional trader з повним ICT-арсеналом.
goal: Ролі R_TRADER і R_CHART_UX покривають весь спектр ICT/SMC концептів для A+ setups.
risks: Нові концепти ще не реалізовані в engine — role specs описують очікування трейдера. Потрібні ADR для sessions та IOFED.

notes: Аналіз engine coverage: fractals=partial (swings.py), sessions=NOT, IOFED=NOT, momentum=NOT standalone, context flow=partial (trend_bias only).

summary: Williams Fractals (P1): display-only fractal markers — period=2, separate from structure chain
details: Option B: detect_fractals() as separate display layer (period=2, Williams 5-bar pattern). Does NOT feed into classify_swings/detect_structure — prevents false BOS/CHoCH noise. Backend: SWING_KINDS +fractal_high/fractal_low, SmcConfig.fractal_period=2, SmcDisplayConfig.max_display_fractals=30, detect_fractals() in swings.py, engine _compute_snapshot + split _filter_for_display (struct vs fractal caps). UI: purple triangle markers (alpha 0.40, 3px), FR toggle button with localStorage, layerVisible.fractals. 7 files.
why: Трейдеру потрібні Williams fractals для визначення локальних екстремумів (swing points) окремо від structure swings.
goal: Display-only fractal markers без впливу на BOS/CHoCH ланцюг.
risks: Fractals at period=2 generate many more points than structure swings — capped via max_display_fractals=30.
notes: 424 tests pass, 0 TS errors. Build ok (172 modules).

summary: P2a Momentum/Displacement: displacement candle markers + momentum score + Bias Banner dots
details: NEW core/smc/momentum.py: detect_displacement() finds candles with body>=1.5*ATR and wicks<30% of range, returns SmcSwing markers (displacement_bull/bear). compute_momentum_score() counts displacements in last N bars per direction. Engine: integrated into _compute_snapshot + 3-way _filter_for_display (struct/fractal/displacement). Wire: SmcEngine.get_momentum_score() -> SmcRunner.get_momentum_map() -> ws_server full frame momentum_map field. UI: green/red filled dots on chart (alpha 0.60, 3px), DIS toggle + localStorage, BiasBanner pills show momentum dots (1-3) per TF. 12 files.
why: Displacement candles show institutional aggression; momentum score per TF enriches bias banner context.
goal: Chart displacement markers + momentum strength in Bias Banner.
risks: Display cap max_display=15 prevents clutter. momentum_map is additive field — backward compatible.
notes: 424 tests pass, 0 TS errors. Build ok (172 modules).

summary: P2+P3+P4: adaptive marker scaling, collision offset, directional momentum dots, toggle compaction, BiasBanner reposition

(2) Momentum wire: {b,r} directional counts instead of max(bull,bear). BiasBanner: colored dots (green=bull dominant, red=bear, gray=chop). (3) Toggle compaction: collapsible SMC panel with trigger button. (4) BiasBanner repositioned to top-right below toggles (clears OHLCV tooltip). (5) U3 fix: displacement dot offset corrected (bull above, bear below).

risks: Minor: old clients receiving new momentum_map format will ignore it gracefully (keys still present, just value shape changes)

summary: ADR-0033 Proposed: Context Flow - Multi-TF Narrative Engine
details: Backend-synthesized narrative layer: NarrativeBlock(mode, headline, bias_summary, scenario, next_area, fvg_context, market_phase) + ActiveScenario(zone_id, direction, entry_desc, trigger, target_desc, invalidation, confidence). 3 alternatives evaluated: A) backend pure narrative.py, B) frontend TS synthesis, C) hybrid structured+template. Choice: Alt A (backend) — determinism, testability, thin UI principle. 5 P-slices: P1 types, P2 narrative.py+tests, P3 FVG-OB overlap, P4 runtime wire, P5 NarrativePanel.svelte. FVG display policy: OB-overlapping FVG hidden (badge only), standalone FVG subdued.
why: Trader needs 1-second answer to Trade or Wait. Current pipeline has all data but no presentation layer for actionable decisions.
goal: 3-second answer: Trade/Wait/Caution + Active Scenario with entry/trigger/target/invalidation
risks: Market phase heuristic is approximate. Narrative in full frames only (not delta). English-only v1.
notes: Depends on: ADR-0024 (engine), ADR-0029 (confluence), ADR-0031 (bias). Next: R_TRADER + R_BUG_HUNTER challenge, then R_PATCH_MASTER implement.

summary: ADR-0033 Implemented: Context Flow - Multi-TF Narrative Engine (5 P-Slices)

P4: smc_runner.get_narrative() + ws_server full-frame narrative injection. P5: NarrativePanel.svelte (collapsed/expanded, auto-collapse 10s) + ChartPane wiring. Feature gate: smc.narrative.enabled=false (default).
why: Trader needs 1-second Trade/Wait answer from SMC data pipeline. All data existed but no presentation aggregation layer.
goal: 3-second actionable decision: Trade/Wait + Active Scenario with entry/trigger/target/invalidation
risks: Market phase heuristic approximate. ATR estimate crude (h-l). Narrative in full frames only (not delta).
